### What carries over from 5b

- The **design restraint lesson**: variety without established meaning reads as noise.
  5a's per-kind icons passed real-content review where 5b's abstract shapes didn't, but
  the burden of proof stays on variety — the `markerVisibility` setting exists precisely
  because even good icons can be more than a leaf node needs.
- The **guide-through-marker-center geometry** (already independently present in 5a).
- **Five cross-experiment findings** (shared-value coupling, `background-position` `%`
  semantics, measure-the-glyph-not-the-wrapper, per-depth vs. per-line adjustments,
  simplification-as-legitimate-outcome) — merged into
  [11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md).
- The **live chevron measurement** approach — 5b measured the fold chevron's reach live
  per render; 5a hardcoded two measured constants. Porting 5b's approach is hardening
  item 1 below.

### Cross-branch bug alert (important beyond this experiment)

5a's round 4 discovered that decorations leak into Obsidian's **nested per-cell table
editors** (a bare word in an actively-edited cell classifies as a "paragraph" and picks up
indentation + a marker *inside the cell*). The fix (ViewPlugins gated on a DOM-ancestry
`isNestedEditor()` check) exists **only on 5a**. The latent leak affects the un-merged 5b
*and the upstream 2b/1 branches* (indentation alone leaks, markers just made it obvious).
No action needed on the chosen path — 5a carries the fix and everything merges through it
— but if the upstream experiment branches are ever revived independently, they need the
same guard.

## Next steps: hardening 5a

Concerns identified in the post-experiment code review (2026-07-19), ranked. None are
architecture-threatening; they're hardening tasks, invariants to protect, and deferred
polish for when 5a graduates from experiment to the real implementation.

1. **Replace the two hardcoded fold-chevron constants with live measurement.** The chevron
   repositioning is a static CSS transform (`translateX(calc(-1 * (var(--to-marker-gutter)
   + 0.425rem - 3px)))`) where `0.425rem` is half the chevron wrapper's measured 22px width
   and `3px` is measured internal dead space. This is the one place 5a violates the
   project's own "read native values live instead of hardcoding compensations" rule
   ([11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md)); a theme or Obsidian update that
   resizes the chevron silently degrades the layout (cosmetic-only failure). 5b's session
   solved the same problem with live measurement — port that approach.
2. **Protect two documented invariants in code review.** (a) DOM injection into widget-atom
   subtrees works because Obsidian never re-diffs those opaque subtrees — an undocumented
   invariant; failure mode on an Obsidian change is re-injection flicker or duplicated
   markers. (b) Never append children into a plain `.cm-line` — confirmed to peg the
   renderer at 100%+ CPU via CM6's mutation-observer feedback loop (documented in
   `decorations.ts`'s module comment). Both must survive future refactors.
3. **Evaluate `app.workspace.updateOptions()` as a replacement for the `forceRedraw`
   off/on mode-toggle hack** (used to refresh `MarginCompensation` after a settings change
   when decoration output is byte-identical). The hack works and its reasoning is sound,
   but toggling a user-visible mode as an internal refresh is fragile if mode toggling
   ever gains side effects. `updateOptions()` is Obsidian's public API for exactly
   "editor-extension-affecting settings changed" — and obsidian-lapel (see the prior-art
   addendum below) confirms the pattern works in the wild: it swaps the registered
   extension array's entry in place and makes this one call.
4. **Consolidate to one shared `parse()`/`decorate()` pass per transaction.** Currently
   three ViewPlugins each re-parse the full document on every update (the 2b baseline did
   it twice). Same asymptotics, tripled constant; fine for normal notes, worth
   consolidating before testing against multi-thousand-line files. Decoration sets are
   also built for the whole document, not the viewport — a further (deferred) option;
   obsidian-lapel demonstrates the standard viewport-limited shape (build only over
   `view.viewport`, rebuild on `docChanged || viewportChanged`), and additionally shows
   that per-line *kind* facts can come from CM6's own incremental `syntaxTree` (via
   `lineClassNodeProp`) with no separate reparse at all — not a fit for our own tree
   depths (our universal-tree semantics aren't in CM6's grammar), but potentially a fit
   for the kind-classification part of the work.
5. **Resolve the `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` violations 5a introduced.** The marker
   widget's inline styles trip `obsidianmd/no-static-styles-assignment` (14 errors) and
   `obsidianmd/prefer-create-el` — the official lint rules this project's "perfect
   scorecard" bar (decision Q1) commits to satisfying. Static styles should move to CSS
   classes; genuinely dynamic ones (`left`, per-kind expressions) to `setCssProps`. Found
   by `npm run lint` on the merged branch, 2026-07-19; pre-existing warnings aside, the
   errors are all in 5a's marker code. **Status (2026-07-19 backfill): resolved on `main`**
   (`npm run lint`: 0 errors) — static styles moved to CSS classes, dynamic ones to
   `setCssProps`, per the plan above. Only one warning remains
   (`obsidianmd/settings-tab/prefer-setting-definitions`, unrelated to marker code — the
   settings tab predates Experiment 5 and was never migrated to the declarative settings
   API), now tracked as its own hardening task
   ([tasks.md item 5.5](../../openspec/changes/outline-decorations/tasks.md)) rather than
   bundled here.
6. **Polish**: `aria-hidden="true"` on the marker SVGs (decorative, screen readers should
   skip them); untested contexts — RTL text, IME composition at line start, community
   themes beyond those exercised.
7. **Accepted design costs, restated so they aren't rediscovered as "bugs" later**: every
   non-list line reserves the 1.25rem marker gutter, so text visibly shifts when toggling
   outline mode; two Experiment-1 invariants were knowingly relaxed (depth-0 lines are no
   longer padding-free; same-depth list items vs. atoms no longer share a column). The
   pure-list invariant (a list with no non-list ancestors renders byte-identical to
   outline-mode-off) still holds — list items reserve nothing.

## Prior-art addendum: obsidian-lapel (2026-07-19)

Reviewed after the head-to-head verdict, before calling the experiment wrapped:
[obsidian-lapel](https://github.com/liamcain/obsidian-lapel) (by Liam Cain, an Obsidian
team member) ships a related idea — per-level heading markers ("H1"…"H6") with a
click-to-change-level menu. Four source files, so the whole implementation was read.

**Mechanism: a third approach neither 5a nor 5b tried.** Lapel renders markers in a CM6
`gutter()` — a dedicated column left of the content area, ordered relative to the
line-number gutter via extension precedence (`Prec.high`/`Prec.low`). Because a gutter
marker lives entirely outside `.cm-content`, *every* hard problem this experiment fought
simply doesn't exist there: no text-layout impact, no gutter reservation, no
`contain: paint`, no fold-chevron collision, no blockquote `::before` conflict, no
readable-line-width margin interaction. The trade-off is structural: a gutter is a flat,
fixed column — it cannot place a marker at the node's own indent depth, which is the whole
point of our design (the marker as a crown on the guide line, at the node's own column).
So it doesn't replace 5a's mechanism for us, but it is the right tool for flat per-line
chrome, worth remembering if we ever add any (e.g. node handles, diagnostics).

**Independent confirmation of the nested-editor leak (5a's round 4).** Lapel hit the exact
same bug class: its gutter renders inside Obsidian's per-table-cell nested editors. Their
fix is a CSS hide (`.table-cell-wrapper .cm-gutters { display: none }`) with a comment
calling it a hack to remove "once there's a proper way to not register the editor
extension inside table cells" — i.e. they too found no official API to scope
`registerEditorExtension`, and their workaround only hides the symptom (the extension
still runs). Our DOM-ancestry `isNestedEditor()` gate is structurally stronger: it stops
the computation, not just the paint.

**Confirmations picked up into the hardening checklist** (items 3 and 4 above):
`app.workspace.updateOptions()` as the settings-refresh mechanism, viewport-limited
building, and CM6's own `syntaxTree` as a kind-classification source.

### Potential follow-ups (deliberately out of Experiment 5's scope)

- **Per-level heading markers (H1–H6).** Considered during the experiment but kept out to
  avoid scope creep; lapel validates the idea in the wild. Under 5a's mechanism this is
  small: carry the heading `level` (already on `OutlineNode`, present iff
  `kind === 'heading'`) through `LineDecorationFact`, and either branch
  `buildMarkerIcon` per level or render a small text label. Lapel's customization
  pattern is worth copying regardless of the visual: a `data-level` attribute plus a
  CSS-custom-property indirection (`--heading-marker`, consumed by `content:`) lets
  themes/snippets restyle markers per level without touching the plugin — our markers
  could expose `data-kind` (and `data-level`) the same way.
- **Marker interactivity.** Lapel's markers are clickable: a `Menu` listing heading
  levels 1–6 (checked state on the current level, `lucide-heading-N` icons) plus a
  "Body" option, dispatching a line rewrite. This is exactly the "future interactivity"
  potential the head-to-head credited 5a's real-DOM mechanism with — a node's marker as
  a click target for outline operations (change kind/level, fold, zoom, structural
  moves). Two caveats from lapel's own code: our `MarkerWidget` currently sets
  `pointer-events: none` + `ignoreEvent() { return true }`, both of which would need
  revisiting carefully (CM6 widget event handling interacts with editor focus/cursor);
  and lapel positions its menu via `Menu.setParentElement`, which is NOT public API
  (they augment `obsidian.d.ts` locally) — a public-API-only equivalent
  (`showAtMouseEvent` alone) needs verifying against our bar first.

## Open question: shrinking only our own added list margin

Raised during Experiment 1's review, not yet decided. The deferred list-hang issue (see [Experiment 1's results](08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md)) is native
Obsidian chrome and explicitly out of scope for direct edits in this experiment. But a
narrower variant stays inside the additive-only discipline: **reduce only the margin *we*
add** to list items — not native `text-indent`/`padding-left` — by the list's own native
hang width, read live via `getComputedStyle` the same way Experiment 1's table fix (bug #3 in
[08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md](08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md))
reads and compensates for native padding. Worth exploring as a follow-up, with two open
risks to resolve before trying it: (a) clamping so a shallow `supplementalDepth` never goes
negative once the hang is subtracted (the table fix's `max(0px, ...)` pattern applies
directly), and (b) the compensation must be based on the list **root**'s own hang, not each
item's — nested items can have wider markers (e.g. `10.` vs `-`) with different native hang
widths, and compensating per-item instead of per-root-chain would reintroduce exactly the
kind of within-list misalignment the wide-numbering fixture exists to catch.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * CM6 adapter for outline mode's decorations. All the pure computation
3	 * (depth/supplementalDepth, per-line guide depths) lives in decorate.ts;
4	 * this module only turns those facts into CM6 decorations/DOM, gated
5	 * per-editor on outline mode via the public `editorInfoField` — same
6	 * gating pattern as keymap.ts's grammarExtension.
7	 *
8	 * Two mechanisms, because Obsidian renders atom kinds two different ways in
9	 * Live Preview:
10	 *
11	 * - Most lines (headings, paragraphs, list items, code fences, plain
12	 *   blockquotes) render as a real `.cm-line` that CM6 lets us decorate
13	 *   declaratively: one class plus one CSS custom property per fact (never
14	 *   an inline shorthand property), so styles.css owns the actual
15	 *   `padding-left`/`margin-left` rules and their units (additive-only
16	 *   indentation, Experiment 1) plus the guide-line gradient (Experiment 2b,
17	 *   see the "Guide lines" section below), PLUS a real CM6 `Decoration.widget`
18	 *   for the block marker icon (Experiment 5a, see below) — CM6's own,
19	 *   supported way to insert extra DOM into a line's content without
20	 *   fighting its own re-render/diffing.
21	 * - Tables, callouts, raw HTML blocks, and horizontal rules are rendered as
22	 *   opaque replacement widgets (`.cm-embed-block`, or `.hr` for the rule) —
23	 *   confirmed live: a `Decoration.line` targeting that line's position has
24	 *   no effect at all (not even a class-merge partial win), because the
25	 *   widget's own `toDOM()` produces the line's DOM wholesale and neither
26	 *   CM6 nor Obsidian threads our decoration's class/attributes through it.
27	 *   These need a direct, imperative DOM patch instead — a `ViewPlugin`
28	 *   that, after each render, sets `margin-left` inline (with `!important`,
29	 *   which always wins for an inline style regardless of what any
30	 *   stylesheet rule does), and appends a marker icon child, on whichever
31	 *   such widgets are currently mounted. Appending a child directly into one
32	 *   of these widgets is safe (proven across this experiment's e2e runs):
33	 *   they're opaque, Obsidian-owned subtrees CM6 never re-diffs internally.
34	 *   The equivalent is NOT safe for a plain `.cm-line`, which CM6 actively
35	 *   owns/re-renders — an earlier version of this code tried appending a
36	 *   marker child directly into `.cm-line`s from this same ViewPlugin (to
37	 *   get live-measured multi-line height for code fences) and it pegged
38	 *   Obsidian's renderer at 100%+ CPU indefinitely, almost certainly CM6's
39	 *   own DOM-mutation observer (used to detect external/IME edits) reacting
40	 *   to the unexpected child and re-triggering updates in a feedback loop.
41	 *   Reverted; plain-line markers stay on the CM6-native `Decoration.widget`
42	 *   path below, which has no such risk.
43	 *
44	 * `MarginCompensation` (below) additionally patches margin-left on BOTH
45	 * mechanisms to account for Obsidian's own "readable line width" feature,
46	 * which applies a `margin-inline: auto`-centering rule to every `.cm-line`
47	 * (any div child of `.cm-content`, in fact) — a *uniform* native base
48	 * margin our own `calc(depth * unit) !important` rule was silently
49	 * *replacing* instead of adding to, confirmed live (a depth-0 heading and
50	 * a depth-1 list item under it rendered with the list-item's own box to
51	 * the LEFT of the heading's, an inverted/negative-looking indentation —
52	 * reported by real-vault testing under a community theme with a narrower
53	 * reading column than the bundled themes, where the effect became large
54	 * enough to notice; the bug itself is present under any theme/viewport
55	 * where that base margin is nonzero, bundled themes included). See its
56	 * own doc comment below for the fix.
57	 */
58	
59	import { RangeSetBuilder, type Extension, type EditorState } from '@codemirror/state';
60	import {
61	  Decoration,
62	  EditorView,
63	  ViewPlugin,
64	  WidgetType,
65	  type DecorationSet,
66	  type PluginValue,
67	} from '@codemirror/view';
68	import { editorInfoField } from 'obsidian';
69	import type { NodeKind } from '../model';
70	import { parse } from '../parse';
71	import {
72	  computeLineGuides,
73	  decorate,
74	  type LineDecorationFact,
75	  type LineGuideFact,
76	} from './decorate';
77	import type { ModeSource } from './keymap';
78	
79	// ---- Guide lines (Experiment 2b: CSS stacked-gradient) ---------------------
80	//
81	// @replit/codemirror-indentation-markers' technique: one `--to-guides`
82	// custom property per line, a comma-joined list of repeating-linear-
83	// gradient layers (one per active ancestor depth), consumed by a single
84	// `::before` — O(1) DOM nodes regardless of depth, no JS pixel measurement,
85	// no overlay layer (contrast Experiment 2a's measured overlay divs).
86	//
87	// `to-decor-atom`/`to-decor-list` lines use `margin-left` (Experiment 1's
88	// fix for the "padding never moves the box" bug), which DOES shift the
89	// line's own box — an earlier version of this code concluded from that
90	// alone that a guide could never render on those lines, since their own
91	// `::before` (position: absolute, relative to the line's own box) can't
92	// reach a shallower ancestor's column. That reasoning had a real bug: it
93	// assumed a background is clipped to the *positioned element's own box*,
94	// but the pseudo-element's own box does NOT have to match its containing
95	// block's dimensions — `left`/`right` can widen it arbitrarily, including
96	// leftward past where the line's box starts. Confirmed live (screenshot +
97	// computed style) that nothing in the ancestor chain up to `.cm-scroller`
98	// clips that overflow (`.cm-content`/`.cm-contentContainer`/`.cm-sizer`
99	// are all `overflow: visible`; `.cm-scroller` is `overflow: auto` but its
100	// own box starts well to the left of any guide column we'd ever need).
101	//
102	// The needed compensation ("how far has this line's own box been shifted
103	// right of the global column origin") is fully known at decoration-build
104	// time for both kinds, with NO live measurement: an atom's own shift is
105	// exactly `depth * unit` (our own margin-left value). A list item's own
106	// shift is exactly `supplementalDepth * unit` (our own margin-left value)
107	// too — confirmed live that Obsidian's native hang (`text-indent`/
108	// `padding-left`, applied to the very same `.cm-line`) contributes NOTHING
109	// to the box's own position: `getBoundingClientRect()` on a list line
110	// showed its box's left edge exactly matching its `margin-left`, despite a
111	// nonzero native `padding-left`/`text-indent` also being present — because
112	// neither property moves a box's own edges (padding shifts content only;
113	// text-indent shifts only the first inline line's content, per the CSS
114	// spec), only `margin` does. So both kinds can widen their own pseudo's
115	// box by exactly `--to-own-shift` units to reach any shallower ancestor's
116	// column, with no measurement beyond the JS constants this module already
117	// computes.
118	const UNIT = 'var(--to-decor-unit, 1.5rem)';
119	
120	function guideLayer(depth: number): string {
121	  return (
122	    `repeating-linear-gradient(to right, var(--text-faint) 0 1px, transparent 1px ${UNIT}) ` +
123	    `calc(${depth} * ${UNIT}) 0 / ${UNIT} 100% no-repeat`
124	  );
125	}
126	
127	function guideBackground(guideDepths: readonly number[]): string {
128	  return guideDepths.map(guideLayer).join(', ');
129	}
130	
131	// ---- Block markers (Experiment 5a: icon markers) ---------------------------
132	//
133	// See docs/research/10-experiment-5-block-markers.md (Experiment 5/5a). A
134	// small, distinct, self-drawn SVG icon per node kind, rendered on a node's
135	// own first line only (never a list item — the native bullet/number already
136	// does that job, same exclusion guides already use).
137	//
138	// Placement exploration (post-review) settled on the icon horizontally
139	// CENTERED on the guide-line column, with a marker gutter reserved
140	// additively so text still clears the icon (the other two candidates tried —
141	// icon's own left edge at the column, and no gutter with the icon's own
142	// right edge at the column — read worse in a real vault and were dropped).
143	// `MarkerVisibility` itself lives in mode-registry.ts (not here), so that
144	// pure, Obsidian-free module can keep defining PluginData without importing
145	// this one (which pulls in the real `obsidian` package for
146	// `editorInfoField`).
147	export type { MarkerVisibility } from './mode-registry';
148	import type { MarkerVisibility } from './mode-registry';
149	
150	/** Anything that can supply decorations needs to say which notes are in
151	 * outline mode (ModeSource) and which nodes get a marker at all — a real
152	 * Obsidian setting, read fresh on every recompute so switching it live (no
153	 * rebuild) takes effect on the very next transaction, the same way toggling
154	 * outline mode already does (see main.ts's refreshDecorations). */
155	export interface DecorationSource extends ModeSource {
156	  readonly markerVisibility: MarkerVisibility;
157	}
158	
159	/**
160	 * Whether a given node's marker should render at all (Experiment 5a
161	 * follow-up — see `MarkerVisibility`'s own doc comment in mode-registry.ts
162	 * for the reasoning). Deliberately does NOT touch the marker gutter
163	 * reservation (padding-left/margin-left) at all — that stays reserved
164	 * uniformly regardless of this setting, so hiding some markers never
165	 * reflows text/shifts indentation; only whether the icon itself is drawn
166	 * in that already-reserved space changes.
167	 */
168	function shouldShowMarker(fact: LineDecorationFact, visibility: MarkerVisibility): boolean {
169	  switch (visibility) {
170	    case 'all':
171	      return true;
172	    case 'with-children':
173	      return fact.hasChildren;
174	    case 'headings-and-paragraphs':
175	      // The only two marker-eligible kinds that can ever have children in
176	      // this tree model — atoms are leaves by construction (see hasChildren
177	      // itself), so `!fact.isAtom` is exactly "heading or paragraph."
178	      return !fact.isAtom;
179	  }
180	}
181	
182	const MARKER_GUTTER_REM = 1.25;
183	const MARKER_ICON_REM = 0.85;
184	// The actual CSS length emitted per line (see lineDecoration()) — a single
185	// source of truth the static CSS rules, the live margin overrides, AND the
186	// marker's own left-offset calc all agree with. Every non-list-item line
187	// reserves this gutter unconditionally (see lineDecoration()'s own
188	// reasoning for why list items don't).
189	const MARKER_GUTTER_CSS = `${MARKER_GUTTER_REM}rem`;
190	const MARKER_ICON_CSS = `${MARKER_ICON_REM}rem`;
191	
192	/**
193	 * Where a marker icon's own LEFT edge should sit, given `targetRelExpr` — a
194	 * CSS length expression for "the shared target column (where the guide for
195	 * this depth renders), relative to the box the marker is about to become a
196	 * child of" (see call sites: block/atom/widget-atom each derive this from
197	 * their own already-established `--to-own-shift`-style formula, so the
198	 * marker automatically stays correct if those formulas ever change). Used
199	 * only by the widget-atom mechanism below (table/callout/hr/html) — the
200	 * plain-line mechanism uses `MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR` instead (see its own
201	 * doc comment for why the two need different math). Centers the icon on the
202	 * target column (the placement exploration's winner — see the module doc
203	 * comment above).
204	 */
205	function markerAnchorLeftExpr(targetRelExpr: string): string {
206	  return `calc(${targetRelExpr} - (${MARKER_ICON_CSS} / 2))`;
207	}
208	
209	/**
210	 * Horizontal placement for the CM6-widget (plain-line) marker mechanism —
211	 * see `MarkerWidget`'s own doc comment for why this uses a fundamentally
212	 * different technique (inline + `vertical-align`, not `position: absolute`
213	 * relative to the line's own box) from the widget-atom mechanism above.
214	 *
215	 * Because the widget is always inserted at the exact position where the
216	 * node's own text starts (CM6 `Decoration.widget` at the line's first
217	 * character, `side: -1`), and — by construction — that text position is
218	 * always exactly `gutter` to the right of the shared target column,
219	 * regardless of kind or depth (that IS the definition of the gutter), the
220	 * needed shift collapses to a single depth/kind-independent expression:
221	 * `iconSize * 0.5 - gutter` (icon centered on the column). Worked through
222	 * concretely for both block (padding-shifted text, unshifted box) and
223	 * atom-plain (margin-shifted box, unshifted-relative-to-box text) — the
224	 * depth terms cancel identically in both cases, confirmed by hand before
225	 * relying on it here (see the git history of this comment for the full
226	 * derivation).
227	 */
228	const MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR = `calc(${MARKER_ICON_CSS} * 0.5 - ${MARKER_GUTTER_CSS})`;
229	
230	const SVG_NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
231	
232	function svgEl<K extends keyof SVGElementTagNameMap>(
233	  tag: K,
234	  attrs: Record<string, string>,
235	): SVGElementTagNameMap[K] {
236	  const el = document.createElementNS(SVG_NS, tag);
237	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(attrs)) el.setAttribute(key, value);
238	  return el;
239	}
240	
241	const STROKE_ATTRS = {
242	  stroke: 'currentColor',
243	  'stroke-width': '1.5',
244	  fill: 'none',
245	  'stroke-linecap': 'round',
246	  'stroke-linejoin': 'round',
247	};
248	
249	/**
250	 * Builds one distinct, self-drawn SVG icon per eligible node kind — via DOM
251	 * APIs directly (element creation + attribute setting in code), not a
252	 * data-URI string. Not a final design; exact shapes are expected to be
253	 * tuned by eye during real-vault review, like every other visual call in
254	 * this project (see the plan's own framing of Experiment 5a).
255	 */
256	function buildMarkerIcon(kind: NodeKind): SVGSVGElement {
257	  const svg = svgEl('svg', { viewBox: '0 0 16 16', width: '100%', height: '100%' });
258	
259	  switch (kind) {
260	    case 'heading':
261	      // A blocky "H": two vertical bars + a crossbar.
262	      svg.append(
263	        svgEl('rect', { x: '3', y: '2', width: '2', height: '12', fill: 'currentColor' }),
264	        svgEl('rect', { x: '11', y: '2', width: '2', height: '12', fill: 'currentColor' }),
265	        svgEl('rect', { x: '3', y: '7', width: '10', height: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }),
266	      );
267	      break;
268	    case 'paragraph':
269	      // Three text lines, the last one shorter.
270	      svg.append(
271	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '4', x2: '14', y2: '4' }),
272	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '8', x2: '14', y2: '8' }),
273	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '12', x2: '9', y2: '12' }),
274	      );
275	      break;
276	    case 'code':
277	      // "</>"
278	      svg.append(
279	        svgEl('polyline', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, points: '6,3 2,8 6,13' }),
280	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '9.5', y1: '2', x2: '6.5', y2: '14' }),
281	        svgEl('polyline', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, points: '10,3 14,8 10,13' }),
282	      );
283	      break;
284	    case 'table':
285	      // 2x2 grid.
286	      svg.append(
287	        svgEl('rect', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x: '2', y: '2', width: '12', height: '12', rx: '1' }),
288	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '8', x2: '14', y2: '8' }),
289	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '8', y1: '2', x2: '8', y2: '14' }),
290	      );
291	      break;
292	    case 'callout':
293	      // Filled alert circle with an "!" bar.
294	      svg.append(
295	        svgEl('circle', { cx: '8', cy: '8', r: '6', fill: 'currentColor' }),
296	        svgEl('rect', { x: '7', y: '4', width: '2', height: '5', fill: 'var(--background-primary)' }),
297	        svgEl('rect', { x: '7', y: '10', width: '2', height: '2', fill: 'var(--background-primary)' }),
298	      );
299	      break;
300	    case 'quote':
301	      // Two opening-quote marks.
302	      svg.append(
303	        svgEl('circle', { cx: '5', cy: '5', r: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }),
304	        svgEl('rect', { x: '4', y: '5', width: '2', height: '4', fill: 'currentColor' }),
305	        svgEl('circle', { cx: '11', cy: '5', r: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }),
306	        svgEl('rect', { x: '10', y: '5', width: '2', height: '4', fill: 'currentColor' }),
307	      );
308	      break;
309	    case 'html':
310	      // An outlined tag/document shape with a folded corner.
311	      svg.append(
312	        svgEl('rect', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x: '3', y: '2', width: '10', height: '12', rx: '1' }),
313	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '9', y1: '2', x2: '13', y2: '6' }),
314	      );
315	      break;
316	    case 'hr':
317	      // A single bold horizontal bar.
318	      svg.append(svgEl('rect', { x: '2', y: '7', width: '12', height: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }));
319	      break;
320	    default:
321	      // Unreachable for list-item (excluded by every caller) — a small dot
322	      // keeps this exhaustive-in-spirit without dead code paths elsewhere.
323	      svg.append(svgEl('circle', { cx: '8', cy: '8', r: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }));
324	  }
325	
326	  return svg;
327	}
328	
329	/** Widget-replaced atom kinds (see the module doc comment) — markers on
330	 * these are injected directly by MarginCompensation, not the CM6 widget
331	 * below (a `Decoration` has zero effect on these elements). */
332	const WIDGET_ATOM_KINDS: ReadonlySet<NodeKind> = new Set(['table', 'callout', 'html', 'hr']);
333	
334	/**
335	 * "top:0; bottom:0; flex-center" positioning for the WIDGET-ATOM marker
336	 * only (table/callout/hr/html) — the plain-line marker (`MarkerWidget`,
337	 * above) uses a different, inline/`vertical-align` technique instead; see
338	 * its own doc comment for why. A widget atom IS its own single, opaque,
339	 * already-full-height DOM element (unlike a `.cm-line`, which can carry
340	 * extra margin/padding a naive "center in the whole box" approach would
341	 * wrongly include), so stretching to its own box height and flex-centering
342	 * is correct here without that risk. Deliberately NOT `top: 50%; transform:
343	 * translateY(-50%)` (an earlier version of this code used that and a
344	 * single-line paragraph's icon visibly sat lower than the text's own
345	 * center) — percentage/transform-based centering is sensitive to CSS
346	 * line-height/leading asymmetry; stretching the box to its full containing-
347	 * block height via `top`+`bottom` and centering the icon inside with flex
348	 * lets the browser's own box layout do the centering, unambiguously.
349	 *
350	 * Everything but `left` is a fixed constant, moved into the
351	 * `.to-decor-marker-icon`/`.to-decor-marker-icon--widget` CSS classes
352	 * (styles.css) per `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`'s `no-static-styles-assignment`
353	 * rule — `left` is the only value that genuinely varies per instance
354	 * (depth/kind-dependent), so it's the only one JS still sets, via
355	 * `setCssProps` onto the `--to-marker-left` custom property the class
356	 * references.
357	 */
358	function applyMarkerLeft(el: HTMLElement, leftExpr: string): void {
359	  el.setCssProps({ '--to-marker-left': leftExpr });
360	}
361	
362	class MarkerWidget extends WidgetType {
363	  constructor(
364	    private readonly kind: NodeKind,
365	    private readonly leftShiftExpr: string,
366	  ) {
367	    super();
368	  }
369	
370	  override eq(other: MarkerWidget): boolean {
371	    return other.kind === this.kind && other.leftShiftExpr === this.leftShiftExpr;
372	  }
373	
374	  /**
375	   * Deliberately NOT `position: absolute` (unlike the widget-atom marker
376	   * below) — an earlier version used `position: absolute; top: 0; bottom:
377	   * 0` relative to the LINE's own (padded) box, and a heading's marker sat
378	   * visibly too high, worse the bigger the heading. Root cause: a heading's
379	   * `.cm-line` box includes real, asymmetric spacing (more margin/padding
380	   * ABOVE the text than below, for visual breathing room from the
381	   * preceding block) that scales with heading level — centering within the
382	   * WHOLE box (spacing included) puts the icon above the text's own visual
383	   * center, more so for a bigger heading's bigger spacing. Fixed by staying
384	   * in NORMAL INLINE FLOW (`display: inline-block`), which aligns relative
385	   * to the surrounding TEXT's own font metrics (the actual heading text run
386	   * this widget sits next to) — completely blind to the outer block's own
387	   * margin/padding, by construction.
388	   *
389	   * `vertical-align: baseline` (the CSS default — set explicitly here so a
390	   * future reader doesn't need to know that), not `middle`: a real second
391	   * bug found in review — `middle` aligns THIS box's own vertical center to
392	   * "the parent's baseline + half the parent's x-height," a formula that
393	   * assumes the aligned box's own height is comparable to the surrounding
394	   * text's x-height. Our icon's height is a fixed, unrelated constant, so
395	   * that assumption doesn't hold — the mismatch put the icon consistently
396	   * low by roughly half its own height, on every kind, not just headings
397	   * (this time correctly *not* scaling with font size, since `middle`'s
398	   * error term doesn't depend on the wrapper's own fixed height — but still
399	   * visibly wrong). `baseline` instead aligns THIS element's own baseline —
400	   * for an inline-block whose only content is a single replaced child (the
401	   * SVG, itself baseline-aligned with no descender by default), that
402	   * resolves to the SVG's own bottom edge — to the surrounding text's
403	   * baseline. That's the same place a bare capital letter or a digit sits:
404	   * the natural, no-extra-math alignment for an icon meant to read as part
405	   * of the text, confirmed live against every heading level and paragraph.
406	   */
407	  toDOM(): HTMLElement {
408	    const wrapper = createSpan({ cls: 'to-decor-marker-icon' });
409	    applyMarkerLeft(wrapper, this.leftShiftExpr);
410	    wrapper.appendChild(buildMarkerIcon(this.kind));
411	    return wrapper;
412	  }
413	
414	  override ignoreEvent(): boolean {
415	    return true;
416	  }
417	}
418	
419	function computeMarkers(state: EditorState, modes: DecorationSource): DecorationSet {
420	  const path = state.field(editorInfoField, false)?.file?.path;
421	  if (!path || !modes.isOutline(path)) return Decoration.none;
422	
423	  const doc = parse(state.doc.toString());
424	  const totalLines = state.doc.lines;
425	  const builder = new RangeSetBuilder<Decoration>();
426	  for (const fact of decorate(doc)) {
427	    // List items keep their fully native marker, untouched (same exclusion
428	    // guides already use); continuation lines never repeat the marker.
429	    if (fact.isListItem || !fact.isFirstLine) continue;
430	    // Widget-replaced atoms have no plain `.cm-line` for a widget decoration
431	    // to attach to — MarginCompensation injects their marker directly.
432	    if (WIDGET_ATOM_KINDS.has(fact.kind)) continue;
433	    if (!shouldShowMarker(fact, modes.markerVisibility)) continue;
434	    if (fact.lineNumber >= totalLines) continue; // stale fact past a shrunk doc
435	
436	    const from = state.doc.line(fact.lineNumber + 1).from; // CM6 lines are 1-indexed
437	    builder.add(
438	      from,
439	      from,
440	      Decoration.widget({ widget: new MarkerWidget(fact.kind, MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR), side: -1 }),
441	    );
442	  }
443	  return builder.finish();
444	}
445	
446	function lineDecoration(fact: LineDecorationFact, guide: LineGuideFact): Decoration {
447	  const styles: string[] = [];
448	  let cls: string;
449	  // Own-shift expression (units of `--to-decor-unit`, plus the marker
450	  // gutter where applicable) this line's own box has been shifted right by
451	  // its own margin-left — the exact compensation the guide's pseudo needs
452	  // to widen its box by, leftward, to reach a shallower ancestor's column
453	  // (see the doc comment above `guideLayer`). `undefined` means the box
454	  // isn't shifted at all (block lines: padding-left never moves the box).
455	  let ownShiftExpr: string | undefined;
456	
457	  if (fact.isListItem) {
458	    cls = 'to-decor-list';
459	    styles.push(`--to-supp-depth: ${fact.supplementalDepth}`);
460	    // List items get no marker gutter (native bullet/number only).
461	    ownShiftExpr = fact.supplementalDepth > 0 ? `calc(${fact.supplementalDepth} * ${UNIT})` : undefined;
462	  } else if (fact.isAtom) {
463	    cls = 'to-decor-atom';
464	    styles.push(`--to-depth: ${fact.depth}`);
465	    styles.push(`--to-marker-gutter: ${MARKER_GUTTER_CSS}`);
466	    // Every non-list line reserves the marker gutter, so the box is always
467	    // shifted by at least the gutter, even at depth 0.
468	    ownShiftExpr = `calc(${fact.depth} * ${UNIT} + var(--to-marker-gutter, 0px))`;
469	  } else {
470	    cls = 'to-decor-block';
471	    styles.push(`--to-depth: ${fact.depth}`);
472	    styles.push(`--to-marker-gutter: ${MARKER_GUTTER_CSS}`);
473	    ownShiftExpr = undefined; // padding-left never shifts a block line's own box
474	  }
475	
476	  if (guide.guideDepths.length > 0) {
477	    cls += ' to-decor-guides';
478	    styles.push(`--to-guides: ${guideBackground(guide.guideDepths)}`);
479	    if (ownShiftExpr) {
480	      styles.push(`--to-own-shift: ${ownShiftExpr}`);
481	    }
482	  }
483	
484	  return Decoration.line({ class: cls, attributes: { style: styles.join('; ') } });
485	}
486	
487	// A blank trailingGap line carrying a guide (see computeLineGuides's doc
488	// comment) has no decorate() fact at all — no depth, no kind, nothing to
489	// indent — so it gets a minimal decoration with just the guide class/style,
490	// not the full lineDecoration() treatment.
491	function gapLineDecoration(guide: LineGuideFact): Decoration {
492	  return Decoration.line({
493	    class: 'to-decor-guides',
494	    attributes: { style: `--to-guides: ${guideBackground(guide.guideDepths)}` },
495	  });
496	}
497	
498	function computeDecorations(state: EditorState, modes: DecorationSource): DecorationSet {
499	  const path = state.field(editorInfoField, false)?.file?.path;
500	  if (!path || !modes.isOutline(path)) return Decoration.none;
501	
502	  const doc = parse(state.doc.toString());
503	  // computeLineGuides is a strict superset of decorate() by line coverage
504	  // (every line decorate() covers, plus gap-only lines) — iterate it as
505	  // the primary sequence (still ascending by lineNumber, required by
506	  // RangeSetBuilder) and look up the matching decorate() fact by line
507	  // number instead of assuming index alignment, since gap lines have no
508	  // corresponding entry there at all.
509	  const factsByLine = new Map(decorate(doc).map((f) => [f.lineNumber, f]));
510	  const guides = computeLineGuides(doc);
511	  const totalLines = state.doc.lines;
512	  const builder = new RangeSetBuilder<Decoration>();
513	  for (const guide of guides) {
514	    if (guide.lineNumber >= totalLines) continue; // stale fact past a shrunk doc
515	    const from = state.doc.line(guide.lineNumber + 1).from; // CM6 lines are 1-indexed
516	    if (guide.isGapLine) {
517	      if (guide.guideDepths.length === 0) continue; // nothing to draw
518	      builder.add(from, from, gapLineDecoration(guide));
519	      continue;
520	    }
521	    const fact = factsByLine.get(guide.lineNumber);
522	    if (!fact) continue; // decorate()/computeLineGuides walks are in sync; defensive only
523	    builder.add(from, from, lineDecoration(fact, guide));
524	  }
525	  return builder.finish();
526	}
527	
528	// Obsidian's own selectors for widget-replaced atom kinds — matches tables,
529	// callouts, and raw HTML blocks (`.cm-embed-block`) plus horizontal rules
530	// (`.hr`, which oddly also carries `cm-line` but is widget-rendered all the
531	// same). Broad by design: elements it catches that don't correspond to an
532	// atom fact (e.g. an inline image embed inside a paragraph) just get their
533	// margin cleared, a no-op.
534	const WIDGET_ATOM_SELECTOR = '.cm-embed-block, .cm-line.hr';
535	
536	// Plain `.cm-line`s that carry one of our own margin-based decorations
537	// (atoms, list items) — needs the SAME native-base compensation as
538	// widgets, for the same reason (see MarginCompensation's doc comment).
539	const PLAIN_MARGIN_SELECTOR = '.cm-line.to-decor-atom, .cm-line.to-decor-list';
540	
541	/**
542	 * Injects/updates the marker icon child on a widget-replaced atom element
543	 * (table/callout/html/hr) — the only way to reach these (Experiment 5a),
544	 * same reasoning as the margin-left patch above: a CM6 decoration has no
545	 * effect on these elements at all. Idempotent: skips the rebuild when the
546	 * kind/position hasn't changed, so a `docViewUpdate` on every render
547	 * doesn't thrash the DOM. Appending a child directly into one of these
548	 * widgets is safe — see the module doc comment for why this is NOT safe to
549	 * do for a plain `.cm-line`.
550	 *
551	 * `ownShiftExpr` is the EXACT expression this widget's own margin-left was
552	 * just computed with (below) — deriving the marker's target column FROM
553	 * that shared expression (`depth*unit - ownShiftExpr`), rather than
554	 * assuming a simplified `-gutter` shortcut, is what fixes a real bug: an
555	 * earlier version used the simplified shortcut for every atom-like kind,
556	 * which happened to match for code/quote (no native-padding correction)
557	 * but silently diverged for tables — whose `ownShiftExpr` ALSO subtracts
558	 * the table widget's own native cell padding (see the margin-left comment
559	 * below) — visibly offsetting the table's marker from every other kind's
560	 * marker at the same depth.
561	 */
562	function applyWidgetMarker(el: HTMLElement, kind: NodeKind, ownShiftExpr: string): void {
563	  const targetRelExpr = `calc(${el.dataset.markerDepth ?? '0'} * ${UNIT} - (${ownShiftExpr}))`;
564	  const leftExpr = markerAnchorLeftExpr(targetRelExpr);
565	  const existing = el.querySelector<HTMLElement>(':scope > .to-decor-marker-icon');
566	  if (existing) {
567	    applyMarkerLeft(existing, leftExpr);
568	    if (existing.dataset.kind === kind) return;
569	    existing.remove();
570	  }
571	  const icon = createSpan({ cls: 'to-decor-marker-icon to-decor-marker-icon--widget' });
572	  icon.dataset.kind = kind;
573	  applyMarkerLeft(icon, leftExpr);
574	  icon.appendChild(buildMarkerIcon(kind));
575	  el.prepend(icon);
576	}
577	
578	function clearWidgetMarker(el: HTMLElement): void {
579	  el.querySelector(':scope > .to-decor-marker-icon')?.remove();
580	  el.classList.remove('to-decor-marker');
581	  delete el.dataset.markerDepth;
582	}
583	
584	/**
585	 * True when `view` is NOT the real, top-level note editor but a separate,
586	 * nested CM6 instance Obsidian mounts inside another widget's own DOM — the
587	 * only case found so far: a table cell currently being edited in Live
588	 * Preview renders as its own tiny, independent `EditorView` embedded inside
589	 * `.cm-embed-block.cm-table-widget` (confirmed live by walking the DOM
590	 * ancestry of a stray marker up to the table widget). `registerEditorExtension`
591	 * (main.ts) applies this whole extension to EVERY CM6 instance app-wide,
592	 * this nested one included, and its own "document" is just the cell's raw
593	 * text — a bare line with no special syntax reads as a plain paragraph to
594	 * decorate()/parse(), so without this guard it picks up a marker AND
595	 * depth-based padding/margin exactly like a real top-level paragraph,
596	 * visibly corrupting the cell being edited. A real top-level note's own
597	 * `.cm-editor` is never itself nested inside a `.cm-embed-block` (those are
598	 * its own descendants, not its ancestors), so this only ever fires for a
599	 * genuinely embedded editor — confirmed also via `editorInfoField`, which
600	 * resolves to the SAME outer `MarkdownView` for both, so state alone can't
601	 * tell them apart; only the DOM ancestry can, which is why this check lives
602	 * here (view-level) rather than in the state-only decoration builders.
603	 */
604	function isNestedEditor(view: EditorView): boolean {
605	  return view.dom.closest('.cm-embed-block') !== null;
606	}
607	
608	class DecorationsPlugin implements PluginValue {
609	  decorations: DecorationSet;
610	
611	  constructor(
612	    private readonly view: EditorView,
613	    private readonly modes: DecorationSource,
614	  ) {
615	    this.decorations = this.compute();
616	  }
617	
618	  update(): void {
619	    this.decorations = this.compute();
620	  }
621	
622	  private compute(): DecorationSet {
623	    if (isNestedEditor(this.view)) return Decoration.none;
624	    return computeDecorations(this.view.state, this.modes);
625	  }
626	}
627	
628	class MarkersPlugin implements PluginValue {
629	  decorations: DecorationSet;
630	
631	  constructor(
632	    private readonly view: EditorView,
633	    private readonly modes: DecorationSource,
634	  ) {
635	    this.decorations = this.compute();
636	  }
637	
638	  update(): void {
639	    this.decorations = this.compute();
640	  }
641	
642	  private compute(): DecorationSet {
643	    if (isNestedEditor(this.view)) return Decoration.none;
644	    return computeMarkers(this.view.state, this.modes);
645	  }
646	}
647	
648	class MarginCompensation implements PluginValue {
649	  constructor(
650	    private readonly view: EditorView,
651	    private readonly modes: DecorationSource,
652	  ) {
653	    this.apply();
654	  }
655	
656	  docViewUpdate(): void {
657	    this.apply();
658	  }
659	
660	  destroy(): void {
661	    this.clearAll();
662	  }
663	
664	  /**
665	   * The margin-left every undecorated `.cm-line` gets natively (Obsidian's
666	   * "readable line width" centering, `margin-inline: auto` under a
667	   * `max-width` — see the module doc comment). Read live, not hardcoded or
668	   * replicated via a calc() formula, since its value depends on the
669	   * current theme/viewport/setting and isn't exposed as a plain-length CSS
670	   * custom property we could reference (`--content-margin`'s own
671	   * *specified* value is literally the keyword `auto`, not a length).
672	   * `.to-decor-block` lines never have their own margin-left touched (they
673	   * use padding), so any one of them is an uncontaminated reference; a
674	   * completely undecorated line (blank gap, preamble) works just as well.
675	   * Falls back to 0 only if the current viewport has neither (e.g. a
676	   * document that is 100% margin-decorated content with nothing else
677	   * rendered) — a graceful degradation, not silently wrong in the common
678	   * case.
679	   */
680	  private nativeMarginBasePx(): number {
681	    // `.hr` is excluded too: it carries `.cm-line` but is widget-rendered
682	    // (see WIDGET_ATOM_SELECTOR) and patched by the loop below — if a
683	    // previous `apply()` call already set its margin-left, querying it
684	    // here would read back our OWN prior value, not the native one.
685	    const ref = this.view.contentDOM.querySelector<HTMLElement>(
686	      `.cm-line:not(.to-decor-atom):not(.to-decor-list):not(.hr)`,
687	    );
688	    return ref ? parseFloat(getComputedStyle(ref).marginLeft) || 0 : 0;
689	  }
690	
691	  private apply(): void {
692	    const path = this.view.state.field(editorInfoField, false)?.file?.path;
693	    // See isNestedEditor's own doc comment — a nested per-cell editor
694	    // shares this.modes.isOutline's own path with the real top-level note,
695	    // so that check alone can't exclude it; only the DOM-level one can.
696	    if (!path || !this.modes.isOutline(path) || isNestedEditor(this.view)) {
697	      this.clearAll();
698	      return;
699	    }
700	
701	    const doc = parse(this.view.state.doc.toString());
702	    const factsByLine = new Map(decorate(doc).map((f) => [f.lineNumber, f]));
703	    const guidesByLine = new Map(computeLineGuides(doc).map((g) => [g.lineNumber, g]));
704	    const nativeBasePx = this.nativeMarginBasePx();
705	
706	    const widgets = Array.from(
707	      this.view.contentDOM.querySelectorAll<HTMLElement>(WIDGET_ATOM_SELECTOR),
708	    );
709	    for (const el of widgets) {
710	      const lineNumber = this.view.state.doc.lineAt(this.view.posAtDOM(el)).number - 1;
711	      const fact = factsByLine.get(lineNumber);
712	      if (fact?.isAtom) {
713	        // Some widgets (tables, for their row/column drag-handles) carry
714	        // their own native left padding that our margin doesn't know
715	        // about — padding never moves a box's own edge, so it just pushes
716	        // the widget's *visible content* (e.g. the <table> grid) further
717	        // right than a same-depth code block or callout, whose background
718	        // fills their own padding invisibly. Reading it live (not a
719	        // hardcoded constant) keeps this correct across themes; clamped at
720	        // 0 so a depth-0 atom never goes negative.
721	        const nativePaddingLeft = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(el).paddingLeft) || 0;
722	        // The widget's own box's rightward shift *from our own
723	        // contribution alone* — this (NOT including nativeBasePx) is also
724	        // the exact compensation a guide's pseudo-element needs to widen
725	        // itself by, leftward, to reach a shallower ancestor's column (see
726	        // the "Guide lines" doc comment above): nativeBasePx applies
727	        // uniformly to every line regardless of depth, so it cancels out
728	        // of the *difference* between any two lines' columns and must be
729	        // added to margin-left but never to `--to-own-shift`. Includes the
730	        // marker gutter (Experiment 5a) — every widget atom always gets a
731	        // marker, so its own box is always shifted by at least the gutter,
732	        // even at depth 0. `applyWidgetMarker` below derives the marker's
733	        // OWN target column from this exact same expression, so the two can
734	        // never silently diverge again.
735	        const ownShiftExpr = `max(0px, calc(${fact.depth} * ${UNIT} - ${nativePaddingLeft}px)) + ${MARKER_GUTTER_CSS}`;
736	        el.style.setProperty('margin-left', `calc(${nativeBasePx}px + ${ownShiftExpr})`, 'important');
737	
738	        // The gutter reservation above stays unconditional regardless of
739	        // markerVisibility — hiding some markers should never reflow text
740	        // or shift indentation, only whether the icon itself is drawn in
741	        // that already-reserved space (see shouldShowMarker's own doc
742	        // comment).
743	        if (shouldShowMarker(fact, this.modes.markerVisibility)) {
744	          el.classList.add('to-decor-marker');
745	          el.dataset.markerDepth = String(fact.depth);
746	          applyWidgetMarker(el, fact.kind, ownShiftExpr);
747	        } else {
748	          clearWidgetMarker(el);
749	        }
750	
751	        const guide = guidesByLine.get(lineNumber);
752	        if (guide && guide.guideDepths.length > 0) {
753	          el.classList.add('to-decor-guides');
754	          el.style.setProperty('--to-guides', guideBackground(guide.guideDepths));
755	          el.style.setProperty('--to-own-shift', `calc(${ownShiftExpr})`);
756	        } else {
757	          el.classList.remove('to-decor-guides');
758	          el.style.removeProperty('--to-guides');
759	          el.style.removeProperty('--to-own-shift');
760	        }
761	      } else {
762	        el.style.removeProperty('margin-left');
763	        el.classList.remove('to-decor-guides');
764	        el.style.removeProperty('--to-guides');
765	        el.style.removeProperty('--to-own-shift');
766	        clearWidgetMarker(el);
767	      }
768	    }
769	
770	    // Plain lines (atoms/list items rendered as genuine `.cm-line`s, not
771	    // widgets): styles.css's static `calc(depth * unit) !important` rule
772	    // already sets the class-driven part correctly, but has no way to
773	    // read/add nativeBasePx (a StateField has no DOM to measure — only a
774	    // ViewPlugin, running after render, can). This overrides it inline
775	    // (inline `!important` beats any stylesheet `!important`, regardless
776	    // of specificity) with the same value PLUS the live-read native base.
777	    const plainLines = Array.from(
778	      this.view.contentDOM.querySelectorAll<HTMLElement>(PLAIN_MARGIN_SELECTOR),
779	    );
780	    for (const el of plainLines) {
781	      const isListItem = el.classList.contains('to-decor-list');
782	      const depthVar = isListItem ? '--to-supp-depth' : '--to-depth';
783	      // List items get no marker gutter (native bullet/number only, no
784	      // icon); atom lines (code/quote) always reserve one, even at depth 0.
785	      const gutter = isListItem ? '0px' : MARKER_GUTTER_CSS;
786	      el.style.setProperty(
787	        'margin-left',
788	        `calc(${nativeBasePx}px + var(${depthVar}, 0) * ${UNIT} + ${gutter})`,
789	        'important',
790	      );
791	
792	      // Marker horizontal compensation: `computeMarkers`'s own `left` calc
793	      // (via MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR) assumes the widget's insertion point has
794	      // zero native rightward shift on this line — true for a plain
795	      // paragraph, but not for two atom-plain cases, both live-verified:
796	      //
797	      // - A code fence's own opener line (`.HyperMD-codeblock-begin`)
798	      //   carries plain native `padding-left` (confirmed live: 16px in
799	      //   bundled themes, presumably reserved for the language-label
800	      //   pill) with no offsetting `text-indent` — shifts the insertion
801	      //   point right by the full padding amount.
802	      // - A blockquote's own line carries the SAME hanging-indent PAIR
803	      //   the original postmortem already flagged for list-item bullets
804	      //   (`text-indent: -13px` matched with `padding-left: 13px`,
805	      //   confirmed live) — the negative text-indent cancels the padding
806	      //   for the line's own FIRST inline position, so naively
807	      //   subtracting `padding-left` alone overcorrects by the full
808	      //   padding amount (confirmed live: produced a new, equal-and-
809	      //   opposite misalignment). Summing `padding-left + text-indent`
810	      //   (text-indent already negative when present) gives the correct
811	      //   net shift in both cases: 16px + 0 for code, 13px + -13px = 0
812	      //   for quote.
813	      //
814	      // Same class of bug the widget-atom fix above already handles for
815	      // tables (a native offset our own formula doesn't know about) — same
816	      // fix here: read it live, never hardcode it, since it depends on the
817	      // active theme. A `querySelector` (not a `decorate()` fact lookup)
818	      // gates this: only a line `computeMarkers` actually placed an icon
819	      // on has one to correct.
820	      const icon = el.querySelector<HTMLElement>(':scope > .to-decor-marker-icon');
821	      if (icon) {
822	        const iconLineStyle = getComputedStyle(el);
823	        const nativeShift =
824	          (parseFloat(iconLineStyle.paddingLeft) || 0) + (parseFloat(iconLineStyle.textIndent) || 0);
825	        // Always SET (never remove) `left` here: this element is the SAME
826	        // node `MarkerWidget.toDOM()` already applied its own base
827	        // `left` to (in the same inline-style object) — `removeProperty`
828	        // would delete that value entirely (falling back to `auto`, i.e.
829	        // no shift at all) instead of restoring it, a real bug caught live
830	        // when a blockquote's own padding/text-indent pair summed to
831	        // exactly 0 and the marker landed with no shift whatsoever.
832	        icon.style.setProperty(
833	          'left',
834	          nativeShift !== 0
835	            ? `calc(${MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR} - ${nativeShift}px)`
836	            : MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR,
837	          'important',
838	        );
839	      }
840	    }
841	  }
842	
843	  private clearAll(): void {
844	    const widgets = Array.from(
845	      this.view.contentDOM.querySelectorAll<HTMLElement>(WIDGET_ATOM_SELECTOR),
846	    );
847	    for (const el of widgets) {
848	      el.style.removeProperty('margin-left');
849	      el.classList.remove('to-decor-guides');
850	      el.style.removeProperty('--to-guides');
851	      el.style.removeProperty('--to-own-shift');
852	      clearWidgetMarker(el);
853	    }
854	    const plainLines = Array.from(
855	      this.view.contentDOM.querySelectorAll<HTMLElement>(PLAIN_MARGIN_SELECTOR),
856	    );
857	    for (const el of plainLines) el.style.removeProperty('margin-left');
858	  }
859	}
860	
861	export function decorationsExtension(modes: DecorationSource): Extension {
862	  return [
863	    // ViewPlugins (not plain StateFields) specifically so each has `view`
864	    // access to run isNestedEditor's DOM-ancestry check — state alone can't
865	    // tell a nested per-cell editor apart from the real top-level note (see
866	    // isNestedEditor's own doc comment). Recomputes on every update, not
867	    // just docChanged ones: toggling outline mode has no doc change of its
868	    // own, only a nudged selection transaction (see main.ts) to make these
869	    // re-run.
870	    ViewPlugin.define((view) => new DecorationsPlugin(view, modes), {
871	      decorations: (v) => v.decorations,
872	    }),
873	    // A SEPARATE plugin for block-marker widgets (Experiment 5a), not
874	    // merged into the same RangeSetBuilder as the line decorations above —
875	    // CM6 merges decorations from multiple sources correctly on its own,
876	    // sidestepping any need to reason about Decoration.line/Decoration.
877	    // widget ordering at the same document position.
878	    ViewPlugin.define((view) => new MarkersPlugin(view, modes), {
879	      decorations: (v) => v.decorations,
880	    }),
881	    ViewPlugin.define<MarginCompensation>((view) => new MarginCompensation(view, modes)),
882	  ];
883	}

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * Outline mode decorations — additive-only indentation (Experiment 1, see
3	 * docs/research/08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md and the postmortem
4	 * it links). `rem`, not `em`: custom properties resolve at the point of use,
5	 * so an `em`-based unit would inflate under a heading's larger font-size —
6	 * exactly the bug that shipped last time.
7	 *
8	 * Selector specificity: `!important` alone is not enough. Obsidian's own
9	 * app.css has `.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > * { margin: 0px
10	 * !important; }` (three classes) resetting margin on every `.cm-line` —
11	 * our `.cm-line.to-decor-*` (two classes) lost to it outright even with
12	 * `!important` on both sides, confirmed live against a real build. Matching
13	 * the same ancestor structure they use is what wins the cascade, not the
14	 * `!important` keyword by itself.
15	 */
16	
17	/*
18	 * `--to-marker-gutter` (Experiment 5a, see
19	 * docs/research/10-experiment-5-block-markers.md): a fixed, additive
20	 * reservation for a per-kind block marker icon, on top of the existing
21	 * depth-based indentation. Applies to every non-list-item line (block and
22	 * atom alike) — list items reserve nothing, since they keep their fully
23	 * native marker untouched. `rem`, never `em`, same reasoning as
24	 * `--to-decor-unit` above. Explicitly set per-line by `lineDecoration()`
25	 * (decorations.ts) — this rule's own fallback (`0px`) only matters before
26	 * that JS has run at all; it is never the value actually in effect.
27	 * `position: relative` gives the marker icon
28	 * (a real, absolutely-positioned DOM child, not a pseudo-element — the
29	 * whole reason Experiment 5a doesn't need to fight over `::before`/
30	 * `::after` with the guide or blockquote's native bar) a containing block
31	 * matching this line's own box.
32	 */
33	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-line.to-decor-block {
34	  position: relative;
35	  padding-left: calc(
36	    var(--to-depth, 0) * var(--to-decor-unit, 1.5rem) + var(--to-marker-gutter, 0px)
37	  ) !important;
38	}
39	
40	/* Atoms (code/table/quote/callout/html/hr) render a visible background or
41	 * border box; padding-left only shifts an element's own content, never
42	 * that box, so atoms need margin-left instead. */
43	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-line.to-decor-atom {
44	  position: relative;
45	  margin-left: calc(
46	    var(--to-depth, 0) * var(--to-decor-unit, 1.5rem) + var(--to-marker-gutter, 0px)
47	  ) !important;
48	}
49	
50	/* List items: native text-indent/padding-left (the bullet hang) is never
51	 * touched. margin-left is a box-model property native list rendering
52	 * doesn't otherwise use, so adding supplemental depth here is genuinely
53	 * additive on top of untouched native positioning. */
54	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-line.to-decor-list {
55	  margin-left: calc(var(--to-supp-depth, 0) * var(--to-decor-unit, 1.5rem)) !important;
56	}
57	
58	/*
59	 * Guide lines (Experiment 2b) — CSS stacked-gradient technique
60	 * (@replit/codemirror-indentation-markers), see
61	 * docs/research/09-experiment-2-guide-lines.md. decorations.ts merges a
62	 * `--to-guides` custom property (a comma-joined list of repeating-linear-
63	 * gradient layers, one per active ancestor depth) into the same line that
64	 * already carries `--to-depth`/`--to-supp-depth` — a single `::before`
65	 * renders every layer as one background, O(1) DOM nodes regardless of
66	 * depth, on every kind (block, atom, list item, AND widget-replaced atoms
67	 * — table/callout/hr/html — which AtomWidgetMargins patches directly via
68	 * the same class/custom-properties, since a `Decoration.line` has no
69	 * effect on those at all, same as its margin-left compensation).
70	 *
71	 * `to-decor-atom`/`to-decor-list` lines use `margin-left`, which shifts
72	 * the LINE'S OWN BOX — an early version of this code assumed that meant a
73	 * guide could never render on those lines at all (a background confined
74	 * to match the line's own box would indeed be stuck there). That was

82	 * `.cm-scroller` clips this leftward overflow.

84	 * Neither `!important` nor Obsidian's own ancestor-chain specificity
85	 * workaround is needed for `position`/`background` themselves (unlike
86	 * padding-left/margin-left above): those aren't contested by any of
87	 * Obsidian's own rules on `.cm-line`/`.cm-embed-block` — confirmed live.
88	 *
89	 * Uses `::after`, not `::before`: Obsidian implements a blockquote's own
90	 * colored side-bar via a NATIVE `::before` (`border-left`, `position:
91	 * absolute; left: 0`, confirmed live via computed style with outline mode
92	 * off) — an early version of this rule used `::before` too, which doesn't
93	 * "double up" with the native one, it completely REPLACES it (an element
94	 * has exactly one `::before`), silently deleting the native bar wherever
95	 * a guide became active on a blockquote line. Confirmed `::after` is
96	 * unused (`content: none`) by every kind this rule targets, including all
97	 * four widget kinds, before switching to it.
98	 */

100	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-embed-block.to-decor-guides {

102	}
103	
104	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-line.to-decor-guides::after,
105	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-embed-block.to-decor-guides::after {
106	  content: '';
107	  position: absolute;
108	  top: 0;
109	  left: calc(-1 * var(--to-own-shift, 0px));
110	  right: 0;
111	  bottom: 0;
112	  background: var(--to-guides);
113	  opacity: 0.6;
114	  pointer-events: none;
115	  z-index: -1;
116	}
117	
118	/*
119	 * Widget-replaced atoms (table/callout/html/hr) DO need one more override,
120	 * and it's a genuinely different mechanism from the padding/margin
121	 * cascade fight above: Obsidian's own app.css sets `contain: paint
122	 * !important` on `.cm-content > [contenteditable="false"]` (confirmed via
123	 * live inspection of matching stylesheet rules) — likely a rendering/
124	 * virtualization isolation hint for these widgets. Paint containment
125	 * clips ALL descendant painting (including a pseudo-element) to the
126	 * element's own box, REGARDLESS of `overflow` — a plain `.cm-line` never
127	 * has this, which is why atoms/list items above needed no equivalent
128	 * rule. `!important` alone isn't enough to beat it (same lesson as the
129	 * padding/margin fight): a 2-class override lost on specificity until it

131	 * test (a probe `::before` was completely invisible at any negative
132	 * `left`, even a single pixel, until specificity was matched — ruling out
133	 * a z-index/stacking explanation, since the SAME probe rendered fine at a
134	 * non-negative offset with no `contain` override at all).
135	 *
136	 * Confirmed safe to remove: paint containment is a pure rendering
137	 * isolation/perf hint with no effect on visual output by itself: removing
138	 * it changes nothing else about how callout/hr/html render (verified by
139	 * screenshot).
140	 *
141	 * Table needed one more fix on top of `contain`: it also carries its own
142	 * `overflow-x: auto` on this SAME outer element (for horizontal scrolling
143	 * of wide tables). Forcing `overflow: visible` here too naively (tried and
144	 * measured live) makes the table's own wide content spill out unclipped —
145	 * and since nothing else up the ancestor chain clips it either, that width
146	 * cascades all the way to `.cm-scroller`, turning the WHOLE document
147	 * horizontally scrollable instead of just the table (confirmed live: the
148	 * rest of the note visibly scrolled out of view together with the table).
149	 * CSS overflow is all-or-nothing per box — there's no way to say "clip my
150	 * own rightward overflow but let this pseudo-element bleed left."
151	 *

153	 * `<table>` (plus its add-row/add-column buttons) in an inner
154	 * `.table-wrapper` div, separate from the outer element this rule targets.
155	 * Moving `overflow-x: auto` onto `.table-wrapper` instead decouples the two
156	 * concerns onto two different boxes: the OUTER element stays
157	 * `overflow: visible` (letting the guide's `::after` bleed left, as below),
158	 * while `.table-wrapper` independently scrolls/clips the actual wide table

161	 * wrapper does, and responds to scrollLeft), AND confirmed by a human
162	 * actually using the table's scrollbar (trackpad/click-drag) in a real
163	 * running vault — no visual or usability defects found. Guides now render
164	 * correctly through every kind in the corpus, tables included.
165	 *

167	 * exact same reason: a widget atom's marker icon is a real DOM child
168	 * positioned outside its own box (`left: -gutter`, see decorations.ts), and
169	 * `contain: paint` clips real descendant elements, not just pseudo-element
170	 * backgrounds — a marker is present on EVERY widget atom (unlike a guide,
171	 * which needs an ancestor), so this can't stay gated on `.to-decor-guides`
172	 * alone, or a depth-0 table/callout/hr/html with no ancestor would have its
173	 * marker silently clipped.
174	 */
175	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > [contenteditable='false'].to-decor-guides,
176	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > [contenteditable='false'].to-decor-marker {
177	  position: relative;
178	  contain: none !important;
179	  overflow: visible !important;
180	}
181	
182	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-table-widget.to-decor-guides .table-wrapper,
183	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6 .cm-content > .cm-table-widget.to-decor-marker .table-wrapper {
184	  overflow-x: auto !important;
185	  max-width: 100% !important;
186	}
187	
188	/*
189	 * Fold-indicator/marker crowding (Experiment 5a follow-up): a heading's
190	 * native collapse chevron (`.cm-fold-indicator .collapse-indicator`) is
191	 * inserted at essentially the same "line start" position our own marker
192	 * icon anchors to, and its own `inset-inline-end: 0` (no extra offset)
193	 * lands it right on top of our marker at every heading level — confirmed
194	 * live via `document.styleSheets` inspection of the actual matching rule.
195	 *
196	 * A first attempt mirrored Obsidian's OWN list-line override for this same
197	 * problem (`padding-inline-end` + a matching negative `inset-inline-end`,
198	 * driven there by a `--list-bullet-end-padding` theme variable) — but
199	 * verified live (screenshot + rect measurement) that this widens the
200	 * chevron's own invisible hit-area rightward without actually relocating
201	 * the visible glyph — the two properties' effects on a shrink-to-fit
202	 * `position: absolute` flex box cancel out for the glyph's own position,
203	 * they don't move it. `transform: translateX()` instead moves the WHOLE
204	 * rendered box — glyph and hit-area together — unambiguously, confirmed
205	 * live to actually place the chevron to the left of our marker instead of
206	 * merely reducing the overlap.
207	 *
208	 * The shift is depth-independent by construction, the same way
209	 * `markerLeftShiftExpr` (decorations.ts) turned out to be: the chevron's
210	 * wrapper is inserted at essentially the same "text start" anchor our own
211	 * marker's target column is defined relative to, so the needed leftward
212	 * distance is always `gutter + half the marker's own width + a small
213	 * visual gap`, regardless of heading depth.
214	 *
215	 * A real spatial constraint, found via a real-vault report (a nested
216	 * heading, not this experiment's flat 2-level fixtures): for a heading at
217	 * depth N > 0, the guide column belonging to its shallowest ANCESTOR (not
218	 * its own depth) also passes through this line, one `--to-decor-unit`
219	 * closer to the marker than the shift above accounts for. Measured live at
220	 * depth 2: ~17px of clear space exists between that ancestor guide and the
221	 * marker's own left edge.
222	 *
223	 * A second real correction, also caught by the user (not assumed from the
224	 * box model): the WRAPPER box (`.collapse-indicator`, 22px, what the two
225	 * paragraphs above size against) is NOT the same as the visible glyph —
226	 * confirmed live via the actual `<svg>` element's own rect and `<path>`
227	 * `getBBox()`: the painted glyph is only ~10px wide, centered inside that
228	 * 22px box (~6px of invisible padding on each side, part of the click
229	 * hit-area but never painted). The 17px gap comfortably fits a 10px glyph;
230	 * the earlier "22px can't fit in 17px" conclusion measured the wrong box.
231	 * `deadSpace` below is that fixed ~6px (native Obsidian sizing, not one of
232	 * our own `rem`-scaled constants — kept as a bare px measured constant, the
233	 * same way `MARKER_GUTTER_REM` itself started as a measured value).
234	 */
235	.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6.is-live-preview
236	  .cm-line.to-decor-block
237	  .cm-fold-indicator
238	  .collapse-indicator {
239	  transform: translateX(calc(-1 * (var(--to-marker-gutter, 1.25rem) + 0.425rem - 3px)));
240	}
241	
242	/*
243	 * Marker icon (Experiment 5a): static styling for both delivery mechanisms
244	 * (decorations.ts's `MarkerWidget` for plain lines, `applyWidgetMarker` for
245	 * widget-replaced atoms) moved here per `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`'s
246	 * `no-static-styles-assignment` rule — only the marker's own `left` offset
247	 * genuinely varies per instance (by depth/kind), so JS only ever sets that,
248	 * via `setCssProps({'--to-marker-left': ...})`.
249	 *

251	 * own doc comments: plain lines rely on inline flow + `vertical-align:
252	 * baseline` to track the surrounding text's font metrics; a widget atom is
253	 * its own opaque, already-full-height box, stretched and flex-centered
254	 * instead) — `.to-decor-marker-icon` carries what's common to both,
255	 * `.to-decor-marker-icon--widget` overrides the rest for the widget-atom
256	 * case.

258	.to-decor-marker-icon {
259	  display: inline-block;
260	  position: relative;
261	  left: var(--to-marker-left, 0px);
262	  vertical-align: baseline;
263	  width: 0.85rem; /* MARKER_ICON_REM, decorations.ts — keep in sync */
264	  height: 0.85rem;
265	  margin-left: -0.85rem; /* -1 * MARKER_ICON_REM */
266	  pointer-events: none;
267	  color: var(--text-faint);
268	}
269	
270	.to-decor-marker-icon--widget {
271	  display: flex;
272	  position: absolute;
273	  top: 0;
274	  bottom: 0;
275	  height: auto;
276	  margin-left: 0;
277	  align-items: center;
278	  justify-content: center;
279	}
