1	/**
2	 * Pure per-line decoration facts for outline mode's additive-only indentation
3	 * (see docs/research/08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md).
4	 *
5	 * Headings, paragraphs, and atoms have no native indentation of their own —
6	 * decorations.ts sets `padding-left`/`margin-left` = `depth × unit` directly,
7	 * which is additive in effect because there is nothing native to add to.
8	 *
9	 * List items are different: Obsidian already hangs their native bullet/
10	 * number via its own `text-indent`/`padding-left` pair, per raw nesting
11	 * level. That pair is NEVER touched here — instead, `supplementalDepth`
12	 * captures only the contribution from non-list-item ancestors (e.g. a
13	 * heading a list sits under), and decorations.ts adds it as `margin-left`,
14	 * a box-model property native list rendering doesn't otherwise use. A list
15	 * with no non-list-item ancestors gets supplementalDepth 0 everywhere in it
16	 * — byte-identical to outline-mode-off, a permanent regression invariant.
17	 */
18	
19	import type { NodeKind, OutlineDoc, OutlineNode } from '../model';
20	import { isAtom } from '../model';
21	
22	export interface LineDecorationFact {
23	  /** 0-indexed absolute line number in the document. */
24	  readonly lineNumber: number;
25	  /** Distance from the document root; top-level nodes are depth 0. */
26	  readonly depth: number;
27	  /** True only for a node's own first line — carries the hang + marker. */
28	  readonly isFirstLine: boolean;
29	  /**
30	   * True for list-item first lines: they already have a native marker
31	   * glyph (bullet/number) that Experiment 1 leaves fully untouched — kept
32	   * for callers that need to identify it, not consumed by decorations.ts.
33	   */
34	  readonly hasNativeMarker: boolean;
35	  /**
36	   * True for atom nodes (code/table/quote/callout/html/hr). `padding-left`
37	   * only shifts an element's own *content*, never its own border/background
38	   * box — invisible for plain text, but atoms render a visible background/
39	   * border box whose edges stay put regardless of padding. Atoms need
40	   * `margin-left` instead, which actually moves the box; decorations.ts
41	   * uses this flag to pick the right CSS property.
42	   */
43	  readonly isAtom: boolean;
44	  /**
45	   * True for every line (first + continuation) of a list-item node. Native
46	   * `text-indent`/`padding-left` must never be touched for these lines —
47	   * decorations.ts applies `supplementalDepth` as `margin-left` instead,
48	   * on top of native rendering.
49	   */
50	  readonly isListItem: boolean;
51	  /**
52	   * Meaningful only when `isListItem` is true (0 and unused otherwise): the
53	   * depth, in the whole tree, of the nearest ancestor list-item that starts
54	   * an unbroken list-item chain (i.e. total tree depth minus depth-within-
55	   * that-chain) — equivalently, how many non-list-item ancestors sit above
56	   * the nearest list root. Constant across an entire nested list, so native
57	   * per-level spacing within the list is untouched; only the list's start
58	   * position shifts by its non-list ancestors' contribution.
59	   */
60	  readonly supplementalDepth: number;
61	  /**
62	   * The node's own kind (Experiment 5, see
63	   * docs/research/10-experiment-5-block-markers.md) — populated straight
64	   * from `node.kind`, constant across all of a node's own lines (first +
65	   * continuation). Used to pick a per-kind block marker; not consumed by
66	   * Experiment 1/2b's own indentation/guide logic.
67	   */
68	  readonly kind: NodeKind;
69	  /**
70	   * True when the node has at least one child (`node.children.length > 0`),
71	   * constant across all of a node's own lines. Atom kinds are leaves by
72	   * construction (`ATOM_KINDS` never parse internals as nodes) so this is
73	   * always `false` for them; list items are excluded from markers entirely
74	   * regardless. Used by the marker-visibility setting (Experiment 5a
75	   * follow-up) to optionally hide markers on leaf nodes.
76	   */
77	  readonly hasChildren: boolean;
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * Walks the parsed tree in document order (a node's own lines, then its
82	 * children — the same layout `nodeAtLine`/`startLine` assume: trailingGap
83	 * lines sit between a node's own lines and its children, and carry no fact
84	 * of their own).
85	 */
86	export function decorate(doc: OutlineDoc): LineDecorationFact[] {
87	  const facts: LineDecorationFact[] = [];
88	  let current = doc.preamble.length;
89	
90	  const walk = (node: OutlineNode, depth: number, listRootDepth: number | null): void => {
91	    const atom = isAtom(node);
92	    const isListItem = node.kind === 'list-item';
93	    // Entering a new list-item chain (this node's parent wasn't one) roots
94	    // it at this node's own depth; continuing a chain inherits the root.
95	    const rootDepth = isListItem ? (listRootDepth ?? depth) : null;
96	    for (let i = 0; i < node.lines.length; i++) {
97	      facts.push({
98	        lineNumber: current + i,
99	        depth,
100	        isFirstLine: i === 0,
101	        hasNativeMarker: isListItem && i === 0,
102	        isAtom: atom,
103	        isListItem,
104	        supplementalDepth: isListItem ? rootDepth! : 0,
105	        kind: node.kind,
106	        hasChildren: node.children.length > 0,
107	      });
108	    }
109	    current += node.lines.length + node.trailingGap.length;
110	    node.children.forEach((child) => walk(child, depth + 1, rootDepth));
111	  };
112	
113	  doc.children.forEach((node) => walk(node, 0, null));
114	  return facts;
115	}
116	
117	/**
118	 * One line's active guide-line ancestor depths (Experiment 2b, see
119	 * docs/research/09-experiment-2-guide-lines.md) — the CSS
120	 * stacked-gradient alternative to Experiment 2a's pixel-measured overlay.
121	 *
122	 * A depth `d` is active on a line when some strict, non-list-item ancestor
123	 * at tree depth `d` sits above the line's own node — i.e. that ancestor
124	 * "owns" a guide, and every line inside its subtree (its own multi-line
125	 * continuations included, but never the ancestor's own lines themselves)
126	 * should render that guide. List-item ancestors never own a guide,
127	 * deliberately: same real-vault finding Experiment 2a made — Obsidian's
128	 * native indent guides already connect one bullet precisely to the next
129	 * within a list, and a guide of ours alongside them either doubles up or
130	 * reads as unevenly spaced against native per-level width.
131	 *
132	 * Unlike Experiment 2a's `computeGuides` (a per-NODE fact requiring a
133	 * two-pass walk to find each ancestor's subtree span), this is a per-LINE
134	 * fact computed in one pass: no subtree-span bookkeeping is needed because
135	 * a single `Decoration.line` only ever needs to know "which ancestor
136	 * guides pass through *this* line," not the full extent of any one guide.
137	 *
138	 * ALSO covers every blank trailingGap (separator) line, not just nodes' own
139	 * content lines — real-vault review found the guide visibly breaking at
140	 * every blank line between blocks (a plain screenshot glance away from
141	 * noticing it, since a 1-line gap reads as "close enough," but a real,
142	 * confirmed regression against Experiment 2a: its overlay is one continuous
143	 * rectangle per guide, spanning gaps between siblings for free, whereas this
144	 * per-line mechanism draws nothing on a line it has no fact for at all). A
145	 * LEAF node's own trailingGap is "the gap before the next sibling in
146	 * document order, at this node's own level" — it gets the SAME guideDepths
147	 * this leaf's own lines had. A node WITH children's trailingGap is "the gap
148	 * before its own first child" instead — already inside that node's own
149	 * subtree, so it gets `childGuideDepths` (the same depths its first child
150	 * gets), not `guideDepths`. An earlier version of this code left the
151	 * "before first child" case uncovered, reasoning it matched Experiment 2a's
152	 * own span (which starts at the first child's own line, not before it) —
153	 * true, but that's an incidental artifact of how 2a's span is computed, not
154	 * a deliberate design goal worth preserving, and it read as a real,
155	 * confirmed break on further real-vault review (the guide visibly stopped
156	 * short right after any heading/paragraph with children). Covering it here
157	 * is a genuine improvement over 2a's own behavior, not just parity with it.
158	 */
159	export interface LineGuideFact {
160	  /** 0-indexed absolute line number in the document. */
161	  readonly lineNumber: number;
162	  /**
163	   * Ascending tree depths of every strict, non-list-item ancestor whose
164	   * guide is active on this line. Empty for a top-level node's own lines
165	   * (no ancestors at all) and for any line whose every ancestor is itself
166	   * a list-item chain (deferred entirely to native indent guides, same as
167	   * Experiment 2a's `deep-nesting` fixture result).
168	   */
169	  readonly guideDepths: readonly number[];
170	  /**
171	   * True for a blank trailingGap line carrying a guide (see the doc
172	   * comment above) — these have no corresponding `decorate()` fact at
173	   * all (no depth, no kind), so decorations.ts can't zip this array with
174	   * `decorate()`'s by index anymore; it keys both by `lineNumber` instead
175	   * and additionally decorates any gap-only line found here.
176	   */
177	  readonly isGapLine: boolean;
178	}
179	
180	/**
181	 * Walks the tree in the same document order as `decorate()` (own lines,
182	 * then children) plus blank trailingGap lines between siblings (see the
183	 * doc comment above) — decorations.ts keys this by `lineNumber`, not by
184	 * array index, since gap lines add entries `decorate()` doesn't have.
185	 */
186	export function computeLineGuides(doc: OutlineDoc): LineGuideFact[] {
187	  const facts: LineGuideFact[] = [];
188	  let current = doc.preamble.length;
189	
190	  const walk = (node: OutlineNode, depth: number, guideDepths: readonly number[]): void => {
191	    for (let i = 0; i < node.lines.length; i++) {
192	      facts.push({ lineNumber: current + i, guideDepths, isGapLine: false });
193	    }
194	    current += node.lines.length;
195	
196	    // This node starts owning a guide for its own children from here on —
197	    // unless it's a list item, which never owns one (see doc comment above).
198	    const childGuideDepths = node.kind === 'list-item' ? guideDepths : [...guideDepths, depth];
199	
200	    // Every trailing gap gets a fact now, for full continuity (see the doc
201	    // comment above): a leaf's own gap uses its own guideDepths; a node
202	    // with children's gap is already "inside" its subtree, so it uses
203	    // childGuideDepths instead — the same depths its first child gets.
204	    const gapGuideDepths = node.children.length === 0 ? guideDepths : childGuideDepths;
205	    for (let i = 0; i < node.trailingGap.length; i++) {
206	      facts.push({ lineNumber: current + i, guideDepths: gapGuideDepths, isGapLine: true });
207	    }
208	    current += node.trailingGap.length;
209	
210	    node.children.forEach((child) => walk(child, depth + 1, childGuideDepths));
211	  };
212	
213	  doc.children.forEach((node) => walk(node, 0, []));
214	  return facts;
215	}
216

content.thinking: I'm setting up a caching layer with a WeakMap that stores parsed document facts, organizing line-specific decorations and guides into indexed maps for efficient lookup.

I'm adding a WeakMap-cached shared computation keyed on the immutable state.doc instance so the three ViewPlugins can reuse the same computed facts, even across non-doc updates where the Text instance persists.

lines: } from './decorate';
import type { ModeSource } from './keymap';
+// ---- Shared per-document fact computation (hardening 5.4) ------------------
+// All three ViewPlugins below need the same pure facts (parse → decorate →
+// computeLineGuides) on every update. Each used to compute them
+// independently — same asymptotics, tripled constant (the 2b baseline
+// already did it twice; 5a added a third). Consolidated here into one
+// computation cached by the *document* (`state.doc`, CM6's immutable `Text`
+// instance): whichever plugin runs first on a given document pays the cost,
+// every other consumer — and every subsequent non-doc update, where CM6
+// reuses the same `Text` instance — gets the cached result. A WeakMap keyed
+// on the `Text` itself (not a doc string) means no invalidation logic and
+// no leak: entries die with the document they describe. Sound because the
+// facts depend on nothing but the document text — mode gating and
+// markerVisibility filtering both happen in the consumers, after this.
+interface DocFacts {
+  readonly facts: readonly LineDecorationFact[];
+  readonly factsByLine: ReadonlyMap<number, LineDecorationFact>;
+  readonly guides: readonly LineGuideFact[];
+  readonly guidesByLine: ReadonlyMap<number, LineGuideFact>;
+const docFactsCache = new WeakMap<Text, DocFacts>();
+function docFacts(state: EditorState): DocFacts {
+  const cached = docFactsCache.get(state.doc);
+  if (cached) return cached;
+  const doc = parse(state.doc.toString());
+  const facts = decorate(doc);
+  const guides = computeLineGuides(doc);
+  const computed: DocFacts = {
+    facts,
+    factsByLine: new Map(facts.map((f) => [f.lineNumber, f])),
+    guides,
+    guidesByLine: new Map(guides.map((g) => [g.lineNumber, g])),
+  docFactsCache.set(state.doc, computed);
+  return computed;
// ---- Guide lines (Experiment 2b: CSS stacked-gradient) ---------------------
// @replit/codemirror-indentation-markers' technique: one `--to-guides`

### D4. Full-document recompute on every relevant transaction, no incremental diffing
Confirmed as built, now across three `ViewPlugin`s rather than the originally-proposed
single `ViewPlugin`: `DecorationsPlugin`, `MarkersPlugin`, and `MarginCompensation` each
independently call `parse(view.state.doc.toString())` on every update — the 2b baseline
already did this twice before Experiment 5 added a third pass. Same asymptotic budget
already accepted for the keyboard grammar and structural commands, tripled constant factor;
fine for normal notes, flagged as a hardening item (consolidate to one shared `parse()`/
`decorate()` pass per transaction) rather than a blocker — see `tasks.md`.
input.new_string:
### D4. Full-document recompute on every relevant transaction, no incremental diffing
Confirmed as built, now across three `ViewPlugin`s rather than the originally-proposed
single `ViewPlugin`: `DecorationsPlugin`, `MarkersPlugin`, and `MarginCompensation` all
consume one shared `parse()`/`decorate()`/`computeLineGuides()` computation per document
(`docFacts()` in `decorations.ts`, cached by the CM6 `Text` instance in a `WeakMap` —
whichever plugin runs first pays the cost; the others, and every subsequent non-doc
update reusing the same `Text`, get the cached result). The hardening pass consolidated
this from three independent full reparses per update (the 2b baseline did two; Experiment
5 added a third). Same asymptotic budget already accepted for the keyboard grammar and
structural commands. Viewport-limited building (obsidian-lapel's shape: build only over
`view.viewport`, rebuild on `docChanged || viewportChanged`) remains a separately-deferred
option, not needed at current document sizes.

input.old_string:
- [ ] 5.4 Consolidate to one shared `parse()`/`decorate()` pass per transaction. Currently
  three `ViewPlugin`s each independently re-parse the full document on every update (the 2b
  baseline already did it twice; 5a added a third). Same asymptotics, tripled constant —
  fine for normal notes, worth doing before testing against multi-thousand-line files.
  Building only over `view.viewport` (rebuild on `docChanged || viewportChanged`) is a
  further, separately-deferred option — obsidian-lapel demonstrates the standard shape, and
  additionally shows CM6's own incremental `syntaxTree` could supply per-line *kind*
  classification with no separate reparse, though not our tree *depths* (our universal-tree
  semantics aren't in CM6's grammar).
input.new_string:
- [x] 5.4 Consolidate to one shared `parse()`/`decorate()` pass per transaction — done:
  `docFacts()` in `decorations.ts` computes parse/decorate/computeLineGuides once per
  document and caches by the CM6 `Text` instance (WeakMap, no invalidation logic — entries
  die with the document; sound because the facts depend on nothing but the text, with mode
  gating and `markerVisibility` filtering both applied downstream by the consumers). All
  three `ViewPlugin`s now share it; non-doc updates (mode toggles, selection nudges) reuse
  the cache too, so they no longer reparse at all. Building only over `view.viewport`
  (rebuild on `docChanged || viewportChanged`) remains a further, separately-deferred
  option — obsidian-lapel demonstrates the standard shape, and additionally shows CM6's
  own incremental `syntaxTree` could supply per-line *kind* classification with no
  separate reparse, though not our tree *depths* (our universal-tree semantics aren't in
  CM6's grammar).

content.subject: 5.4 Consolidate to one shared parse()/decorate() pass
content.description: Refactor decorations.ts so the three ViewPlugins share one cached parse/decorate/computeLineGuides computation per doc (WeakMap on state.doc). No behavior change; full suite is the safety net.

content.subject: 5.1 Live-measure fold-chevron constants
content.description: Replace 0.425rem/3px hardcoded constants in styles.css chevron translateX with live measurement (glyph, not wrapper), following MarginCompensation's nativeMarginBasePx pattern. Reach stays unconditional on headings.

content.subject: 5.3 Evaluate updateOptions() vs forceRedraw
content.description: Try app.workspace.updateOptions() in setMarkerVisibility; bar = table-only note's marker updates without mode toggle (existing e2e tests cover it). Replace if it passes; else document and keep hack. Table-only regression test stays either way.

content.subject: 5.5 Adopt declarative settings API
content.description: getSettingDefinitions() + getControlValue/setControlValue on TrueOutlinerSettingTab; clears the last lint warning.

content.subject: 5.6 aria-hidden + RTL/IME/theme verification passes
content.description: aria-hidden="true" on marker SVGs; verification (not necessarily fixes) for RTL, IME composition at line start, 2-3 community themes; record findings as tasks/research note, fix only cheap+safe.
content.activeForm: Polishing accessibility and verifying edge contexts

content.subject: 5.2 Guard the two DOM invariants (lint rule)
content.description: Protect (a) widget-subtree injection assumption, (b) never append into plain .cm-line — via a scoped no-restricted-syntax lint rule for decorations.ts with sanctioned call sites explicitly disabled inline.

content.subject: 4.1 + 4.2 Traceability e2e tests
content.description: 4.1: e2e test that opens a table cell for editing and asserts the nested editor carries no decoration state. 4.2: e2e test snapshotting cursor + undo depth across a decoration recompute (mode toggle), asserting no mutation.

content.subject: 5.8 Restate accepted design costs in design.md
content.description: Add accepted costs (gutter shift on toggle, relaxed Experiment-1 invariants) to design.md Risks so they aren't rediscovered as bugs; check off task.

content.subject: Final verification + draft PR
content.description: build:plugin, full desktop e2e, mobile e2e (triage per buckets), screenshots both themes, lint 0 errors, update doc 10 checklist statuses, tasks.md checkoffs, draft PR to main flagging manual real-vault pass.

content.thinking:
The decoration e2e specs are passing, so I'm moving on to task 5.1 to work on chevron live measurement. I'm adjusting the CSS to shift the collapse indicator by translating it based on the marker gutter width and some fixed offsets.

Now I'm clarifying the semantics: the chevron wrapper sits at the text start anchor, and I need to position the glyph to the left of the marker using the gutter width plus half the wrapper width minus some dead space. The comments and documentation have conflicting details about what exactly `0.425rem` and `3px` represent, but the goal is clear—I need to replace these hardcoded values with live measurements of the actual `.collapse-indicator.collapse-icon` glyph instead of relying on the zero-width `.cm-fold-indicator` wrapper. So the chevron wrapper sits just left of the text start, while the marker icon is centered on the guide column (which is `gutter` left of text start). The marker spans from `col − 0.425rem` to `col + 0.425rem`, where `col` is the column position. I need to work through the geometry of how these elements actually position relative to each other. The actual value is 3px, not 6px as mentioned in the comment—there's some inconsistency in the documentation. The overlap logic seems to be allowing the chevron box to overlap the marker by the difference between the dead space and the visual gap, but I need to reconcile what the styles actually define versus what the comments claim.

The user wants me to replace those two hardcoded constants with measured values following the `nativeMarginBasePx` pattern: measure the chevron wrapper and its glyph on each render, calculate the actual dead space between them, and apply that dynamically instead of relying on static CSS values. Rather than computing the untransformed position (which requires reading the transform matrix), I'll keep the formula structure but measure the two native values directly: the half-width of the marker icon itself (threading it from the shared constant) and a live-measured dead space compensation value that accounts for the actual spacing.

The key insight is that if I'm currently using a fixed 3px dead space value but the actual measured dead space is around 6px, switching to the live measurement would shift the chevron position and change the gap between the glyph and marker—so I need to run an e2e test to ground truth what the actual values are in the real environment. So the wrapper box without transform spans from A minus 22px to A, and the glyph itself sits within that with about 6px margins on each side, making it roughly 10px wide. When I apply the transform shift of 23.8px, the wrapper moves left and the glyph's right edge ends up at A minus 29.8px. The marker is centered at column A minus 20px (the gutter width).

The marker's left edge is at A minus 26.8px (accounting for its half-width of 6.8px), which means there's a 3px gap between the glyph's right edge and the marker's left edge. This gap comes from the formula where the shift amount accounts for the gutter, marker half-width, and the desired gap, all adjusted by the glyph's right-side offset. The current "3px" constant in the code is actually baking together the measured right offset of the glyph with the target gap value.

The key insight is that I can measure the dead space directly from the live DOM elements without needing untransformed positions — since both the wrapper and glyph shift together via transform, the width difference between them stays constant. So the formula becomes: shift = gutter + (marker width / 2) + desired gap - measured right offset, where the measured offset comes from comparing the wrapper and glyph rectangles. This means I can thread the marker width from the existing constant and keep the gutter as a CSS variable, then implement the measurement in the decorations module by sampling a representative line element.

The e2e tests should hold with the gap fixed at 3px, and I need to set the custom property on `view.contentDOM` so it inherits down to the `.collapse-indicator` elements. Updating CSS variables to sync the marker icon size across both the chevron transform and the decorator element, using a fallback to the current 0.85rem value and adjusting the negative margin with calc to match. The question is which element should have this CSS variable set by the MarginCompensation logic that runs per Editor.

Setting it on contentDOM makes sense since the chevron lives inside `.cm-line` which is inside contentDOM, so it'll inherit properly. I should also clean up these custom properties in clearAll for tidiness, though leaving them stale is harmless. For measuring, I'll capture one representative chevron element per render and apply that value uniformly, with the 6px fallback only kicking in before the first measurement. On mobile the same approach applies.

The chevron element exists in the DOM even when not hovered—it's just transparent—which means it's measurable via getBoundingClientRect. Since transforms don't affect width, the distance calculation between the wrapper's right edge and the SVG's right edge stays consistent. I need to guard against the SVG not being loaded yet by only setting the property when both bounding rects have width greater than zero. input.new_string:
* A second real correction, also caught by manual review (not assumed from
 * the box model): the WRAPPER box (`.collapse-indicator`, 22px, what the
 * two paragraphs above size against) is NOT the same as the visible glyph —
 * confirmed live via the actual `<svg>` element's own rect and `<path>`
 * `getBBox()`: the painted glyph is only ~10px wide, centered inside that
 * 22px box (~6px of invisible padding on each side, part of the click
 * hit-area but never painted). The 17px gap comfortably fits a 10px glyph;
 * the earlier "22px can't fit in 17px" conclusion measured the wrong box.
 *
 * The shift decomposes as: marker gutter + half our marker icon's width
 * (so the chevron clears the icon centered on the guide column) + a small
 * fixed visual gap (3px, a design choice of ours) − the chevron's own
 * right-side dead space (that invisible ~6px between its box's right edge
 * and the painted glyph — overlap the marker by that much and the GLYPH
 * still clears it). The dead space is the one genuinely NATIVE measurement
 * here, so it's read live per render by `MarginCompensation.measureChevron`
 * (decorations.ts) into `--to-chevron-dead-right` — hardening 5.1,
 * replacing a hardcoded measured constant that would silently drift if a
 * theme/Obsidian update resized the chevron. `--to-marker-icon-size` is
 * likewise set from its JS source of truth (`MARKER_ICON_CSS`). The
 * fallbacks below reproduce the previously-validated bundled-theme values
 * exactly (0.85rem/2 + 3px − 6px = the old 0.425rem − 3px), so rendering
 * is unchanged until the first measurement lands.
 */
.markdown-source-view.mod-cm6.is-live-preview
  .cm-line.to-decor-block
  .cm-fold-indicator
  .collapse-indicator {
  transform: translateX(
    calc(
      -1 *
        (
          var(--to-marker-gutter, 1.25rem) + var(--to-marker-icon-size, 0.85rem) / 2 + 3px -
            var(--to-chevron-dead-right, 6px)
        )
    )
  );
}

  likewise threaded from its JS source of truth (`MARKER_ICON_CSS`), which also replaced
  styles.css's "keep in sync" duplicate of the icon size. CSS fallbacks reproduce the
  previously-validated bundled-theme values exactly. The chevron e2e test now additionally
  asserts the live-measured property landed (not just that the resulting geometry is
  right), so a silently-dead measurement path can't hide behind the fallback.

content.subject: 5.4 Consolidate to one shared parse()/decorate() pass
content.description: Refactor decorations.ts so the three ViewPlugins share one cached parse/decorate/computeLineGuides computation per doc (WeakMap on state.doc). No behavior change; full suite is the safety net.

content.subject: 5.1 Live-measure fold-chevron constants
content.description: Replace 0.425rem/3px hardcoded constants in styles.css chevron translateX with live measurement (glyph, not wrapper), following MarginCompensation's nativeMarginBasePx pattern. Reach stays unconditional on headings.

content.subject: 5.3 Evaluate updateOptions() vs forceRedraw
content.description: Try app.workspace.updateOptions() in setMarkerVisibility; bar = table-only note's marker updates without mode toggle (existing e2e tests cover it). Replace if it passes; else document and keep hack. Table-only regression test stays either way.

content.subject: 5.5 Adopt declarative settings API
content.description: getSettingDefinitions() + getControlValue/setControlValue on TrueOutlinerSettingTab; clears the last lint warning.

content.subject: 5.6 aria-hidden + RTL/IME/theme verification passes
content.description: aria-hidden="true" on marker SVGs; verification (not necessarily fixes) for RTL, IME composition at line start, 2-3 community themes; record findings as tasks/research note, fix only cheap+safe.
content.activeForm: Polishing accessibility and verifying edge contexts

content.subject: 5.2 Guard the two DOM invariants (lint rule)
content.description: Protect (a) widget-subtree injection assumption, (b) never append into plain .cm-line — via a scoped no-restricted-syntax lint rule for decorations.ts with sanctioned call sites explicitly disabled inline.

content.subject: 4.1 + 4.2 Traceability e2e tests
content.description: 4.1: e2e test that opens a table cell for editing and asserts the nested editor carries no decoration state. 4.2: e2e test snapshotting cursor + undo depth across a decoration recompute (mode toggle), asserting no mutation.

content.subject: 5.8 Restate accepted design costs in design.md
content.description: Add accepted costs (gutter shift on toggle, relaxed Experiment-1 invariants) to design.md Risks so they aren't rediscovered as bugs; check off task.

content.subject: Final verification + draft PR
content.description: build:plugin, full desktop e2e, mobile e2e (triage per buckets), screenshots both themes, lint 0 errors, update doc 10 checklist statuses, tasks.md checkoffs, draft PR to main flagging manual real-vault pass.

content.thinking:
The `updateOptions()` method isn't meeting the requirements — the table-only test with children failed as expected, with the marker remaining on the note even though it should have been removed. There are also some timeout failures that might be cascading from state pollution. The issue stems from how `updateOptions()` triggers a reconfigure transaction, causing `DecorationsPlugin` and `MarkersPlugin` to recompute their state.

However, when dealing with a table-only note, their output stays identical at the byte level, so CM6 never detects a decoration difference and never calls `MarginCompensation.docViewUpdate` — the only mechanism that actually patches widget-atom markers. This is precisely the scenario the `forceRedraw` hack was designed to handle.

So I'm reverting the swap and keeping the original hack in place, then documenting the evaluation results in tasks.md with the evidence from these test failures. The regression test for the table-only scenario is already there in the two tests that just failed.

I've confirmed that `updateOptions()` fails the test—the marker wasn't removed from the table-only case because the decoration plugins produce identical output, so CM6 never triggers the `docViewUpdate` hook that patches the markers. I'll revert the swap and document this finding.

file.content:
* marker visibility silently failed to update until this fix.
   *
   * Toggling outline mode off then immediately back on (via the registry
   * directly, not `toggleMode` — no user-facing Notice for an internal
   * refresh) guarantees two GENUINELY different decoration outputs
   * (`Decoration.none` vs. the real thing) regardless of note content,
   * which CM6 always detects as a real change — reliably triggering
   * `docViewUpdate` twice, with the second pass reading the just-saved
   * setting. Both toggles are public-API-only (an `Editor.setCursor` per
   * step, same trick `refreshDecorations` already uses) — no private CM6
   * access, consistent with this project's own public-API-only bar.
   */
  private async forceRedraw(): Promise<void> {
    const view = this.app.workspace.getActiveViewOfType(MarkdownView);
    const path = view?.file?.path;
    if (!view || !path || !this.registry.isOutline(path)) return; // nothing rendered to refresh
    await this.registry.toggle(path); // off
    view.editor.setCursor(view.editor.getCursor());
    await this.registry.toggle(path); // back on, now reading the new setting
    view.editor.setCursor(view.editor.getCursor());
  }

- [ ] 5.3 Evaluate `app.workspace.updateOptions()` as a replacement for the `forceRedraw`
  off/on mode-toggle hack currently used to refresh `MarginCompensation` after a settings
  change when decoration output is byte-identical (e.g. `markerVisibility` changing on a
  table-only note). The hack works and its reasoning is sound, but toggling a user-visible
  mode as an internal refresh mechanism is fragile if mode toggling ever gains side effects.
  `updateOptions()` is Obsidian's public API for "editor-extension-affecting settings
  changed"; obsidian-lapel confirms the pattern works in the wild.

  off/on mode-toggle hack — evaluated, **rejected with evidence; the hack stays**. Swapped
  in and run against the marker-visibility e2e suite: `updateOptions()` fails exactly the
  scenario the hack exists for (a `markerVisibility` change on a table-only note — the
  byte-identical-decoration-output case — left the widget-atom marker stale; the table-only
  'with-children' test failed with the marker still present). Mechanism: its reconfigure
  transaction re-runs the decoration plugins, but with byte-identical output CM6 correctly
  sees no decoration diff and never fires `MarginCompensation.docViewUpdate`. obsidian-
  lapel's use works because lapel swaps its registered extension array entry in place (a
  real reconfigure diff); our extension instance is unchanged and reads the setting live,
  so there is no diff to see. Recorded in `forceRedraw`'s own doc comment so it isn't
  re-tried; the table-only e2e tests remain the regression net for the scenario.

  mode as an internal refresh mechanism is fragile if mode toggling ever gains side effects.
  `updateOptions()` is Obsidian's public API for "editor-extension-affecting settings
  changed"; obsidian-lapel confirms the pattern works in the wild.

content.subject: 5.4 Consolidate to one shared parse()/decorate() pass
content.description: Refactor decorations.ts so the three ViewPlugins share one cached parse/decorate/computeLineGuides computation per doc (WeakMap on state.doc). No behavior change; full suite is the safety net.

content.subject: 5.1 Live-measure fold-chevron constants
content.description: Replace 0.425rem/3px hardcoded constants in styles.css chevron translateX with live measurement (glyph, not wrapper), following MarginCompensation's nativeMarginBasePx pattern. Reach stays unconditional on headings.

content.subject: 5.3 Evaluate updateOptions() vs forceRedraw
content.description: Try app.workspace.updateOptions() in setMarkerVisibility; bar = table-only note's marker updates without mode toggle (existing e2e tests cover it). Replace if it passes; else document and keep hack. Table-only regression test stays either way.

content.subject: 5.5 Adopt declarative settings API
content.description: getSettingDefinitions() + getControlValue/setControlValue on TrueOutlinerSettingTab; clears the last lint warning.

content.subject: 5.6 aria-hidden + RTL/IME/theme verification passes
content.description: aria-hidden="true" on marker SVGs; verification (not necessarily fixes) for RTL, IME composition at line start, 2-3 community themes; record findings as tasks/research note, fix only cheap+safe.
content.activeForm: Polishing accessibility and verifying edge contexts

content.subject: 5.2 Guard the two DOM invariants (lint rule)
content.description: Protect (a) widget-subtree injection assumption, (b) never append into plain .cm-line — via a scoped no-restricted-syntax lint rule for decorations.ts with sanctioned call sites explicitly disabled inline.

content.subject: 4.1 + 4.2 Traceability e2e tests
content.description: 4.1: e2e test that opens a table cell for editing and asserts the nested editor carries no decoration state. 4.2: e2e test snapshotting cursor + undo depth across a decoration recompute (mode toggle), asserting no mutation.

content.subject: 5.8 Restate accepted design costs in design.md
content.description: Add accepted costs (gutter shift on toggle, relaxed Experiment-1 invariants) to design.md Risks so they aren't rediscovered as bugs; check off task.

content.subject: Final verification + draft PR
content.description: build:plugin, full desktop e2e, mobile e2e (triage per buckets), screenshots both themes, lint 0 errors, update doc 10 checklist statuses, tasks.md checkoffs, draft PR to main flagging manual real-vault pass.

content.thinking:
The functional tests are passing now after the revert, but there are three failures in the screenshot tests due to renderer timeouts—a known issue on memory-constrained machines that's documented in the task notes, though those same screenshots passed in previous runs.

The third failure appears to be a cascade effect from the earlier timeouts: the test creates a fresh note with outline mode off, but if the prior screenshot test timed out mid-execution and left the system in an inconsistent state, the active editor might still be pointing to an old note with outline mode enabled. Since this same spec passed completely earlier (19/19 tests, twice), the best approach is to rerun it in isolation after a brief pause to confirm these are transient renderer issues rather than actual test failures.
The functional tests pass after the revert, with the 3 failures being chromedriver screenshot timeouts—the documented local machine artifact. I'll rerun the spec in isolation to confirm it's stable.

content.thinking:
The test failures are persisting across multiple runs, but they passed earlier before I made the updateOptions changes. After reverting to restore forceRedraw, the code should match the earlier working state, yet something's still off — I need to verify I actually rebuilt the plugin after the revert since that could explain why the tests are still failing.

The screenshot timeouts might be environmental, but the consistent "draws no markers with outline mode off" failure is more concerning. I'm wondering if the vault's plugin data has stale outlinePaths persisted from the failed updateOptions run, even though before() should reset everything. The real puzzle is why a marker icon appears in the active editor when outline mode is off — either the note creation failed and left the previous note active, or decorations are somehow still rendering despite the mode being disabled.

The screenshot timeouts are the bigger issue though. The renderer is unresponsive for over 10 seconds, which suggests something in my changes is causing a performance problem. I'm suspecting the `measureChevron` function that sets CSS custom properties on the content DOM — if CM6's mutation observer is watching for attribute changes on contentDOM itself, setting an inline style could trigger a feedback loop where the observer fires, applies updates, and re-triggers the same mutation, pegging the renderer. So the 19/19 green run did include the measureChevron changes, which is interesting. The loop might be intermittent though — docViewUpdate only fires when the view actually changes, so a style mutation on contentDOM observed by CM6 might not always trigger a full re-render cycle. Or these renderer timeouts could just be environmental degradation from running multiple Obsidian sessions back-to-back, which task 5.7 already documented as causing screenshot timeouts under memory pressure.

To figure out which it is, I should check system load or test the same spec on main to see if screenshots timeout there too. Since my tree is clean except for some uncommitted edits to main.ts, I can stash those changes and test main directly to isolate whether this is a code issue or just local system load.

Actually, before spinning up a worktree, let me look at the timing pattern more carefully. The passing runs earlier today (both 19/19, around 26 seconds) happened before and after the chevron code was added, so the chevron changes aren't causing the slowdown. But the failing runs are taking over 2 minutes with screenshots timing out at 10 seconds each — that's a huge difference from the 26-second baseline.

Since the only code change between the last green run and now was the main.ts experiment (which I reverted), the plugin binary should be functionally identical to what passed. This points to environmental degradation rather than a code issue. The documented precedent from 5.7 showed the same code passing cleanly in isolated reruns, but my reruns are failing — the key difference is system load. Let me check memory pressure and clean up any zombie Obsidian or chromedriver processes left over from the timed-out runs, since those are likely consuming CPU and RAM.

I'm seeing persistent failures only in screenshot-heavy tests and one cascade, but the binary is functionally identical to the one that passed 19/19 earlier—this matches the documented local artifact from task 5.7 about chromedriver timeouts under load. I suspect zombie Obsidian/chromedriver processes from the timed-out runs are the culprit, so I'll check for those now.

content.thinking:
There are some leftover Obsidian processes running—likely the user's own instance from /Applications/Obsidian.app, while the test framework has its own cached version. Memory usage looks fine at 44% free, so the environment isn't under pressure. This suggests the failure might be legitimate rather than environmental. I'm wondering if my chevron styling change could be causing a screenshot mismatch.

Actually, I think I've found the real issue. My `apply()` function now calls `setCssProps` on contentDOM with every document view update, and CodeMirror 6's DOM observer is watching that contentDOM element with attribute changes enabled. This means every time I set the style attribute, it triggers a mutation that CodeMirror detects and processes. If that processing triggers another document view update, it could create a loop. I need to check how CodeMirror's mutation observer actually handles attribute changes on the contentDOM itself—whether it ignores them or if they genuinely cause cascading updates.

The key question is whether setting the same CSS property value repeatedly generates mutation records that keep firing, or if the browser's CSSOM layer is smart enough to skip the attribute update when the value hasn't actually changed. If it's the former, I'm stuck in a render loop that pegs the renderer and causes screenshot timeouts.

The intermittent nature—passing once, then failing—points to something marginal like subpixel jitter. Measuring element positions across renders could oscillate between values like 5.999 and 6.001 pixels due to zoom or scaling, causing the attribute to genuinely change each time, which triggers another mutation and remeasure cycle.

The safer approach is to avoid mutating the observed contentDOM altogether and instead set the custom properties on an ancestor outside of it, like the editor wrapper, where CSS inheritance handles the rest. So the margin-left styles on widget elements inside contentDOM have been stable because setting identical values repeatedly doesn't retrigger mutations. But this new dead-right measurement is oscillating due to subpixel rounding — the getBoundingClientRect values after transform are shifting slightly, creating feedback where the measurement itself feeds back into the value it's measuring.

The fix is to move those properties outside the observer root and round the measured values to a couple decimal places, only writing when the value actually changes. That should break the loop. This also explains why the marker test fails when the renderer gets stuck — the outline-mode note persists with markers still present. The chevron validation passed before because the jitter didn't manifest at that zoom level or timing. Let me implement this now.

I found the issue: `measureChevron` is writing style attributes to `contentDOM` on every update, triggering a feedback loop of mutations and remeasures that pegs the renderer. I'll fix it by writing to the outer `view.dom` instead, rounding measurements, and only writing when values actually change.

content.description: Replace 0.425rem/3px hardcoded constants in styles.css chevron translateX with live measurement (glyph, not wrapper), following MarginCompensation's nativeMarginBasePx pattern. Reach stays unconditional on headings.

content.description: Try app.workspace.updateOptions() in setMarkerVisibility; bar = table-only note's marker updates without mode toggle (existing e2e tests cover it). Replace if it passes; else document and keep hack. Table-only regression test stays either way.

content.description: getSettingDefinitions() + getControlValue/setControlValue on TrueOutlinerSettingTab; clears the last lint warning.

content.subject: 4.1 + 4.2 Traceability e2e tests

content.description: Try app.workspace.updateOptions() in setMarkerVisibility; bar = table-only note's marker updates without mode toggle (existing e2e tests cover it). Replace if it passes; else document and keep hack. Table-only regression test stays either way.

content.thinking:
A commit from earlier today that was passing is now failing the same three screenshot tests with an identical error signature, which tells me this is an environmental issue with the test machine rather than a code problem. The file state notes are just artifacts from the stash operations, not actual changes to the codebase.

My observer-loop hardening is still a valid defensive improvement—writing to view.dom instead of contentDOM avoids the observed subtree and is strictly safer—but I need to correct my documentation since the old code fails identically, which means my original theory about reproducing a pegged renderer was wrong. I should update the comment to honestly describe the actual risk (observed attributes and potential loops) rather than claiming I'd already fixed something.

Once I restore my branch and fix that comment, I'll need to handle the screenshot verification differently given the machine's degraded state. The precedent from 5.7 suggests accepting that local screenshot tests may time out on a memory-pressured machine while relying on CI to confirm they pass on a fresh runner—I should check if this repo has CI workflows set up. I could also investigate whether orphaned Obsidian processes are accumulating or if the wdio config is disabling GPU, which might explain the repeated screenshot timeouts.

Actually, looking at the pattern more carefully: functional tests pass fine (executeObsidian works, renderer responds), but saveScreenshot consistently times out with "Timed out receiving message from renderer." That's a classic Electron/chromedriver issue when the window is occluded or the display sleeps—and given it's late evening, the user's screen likely locked mid-session, which would explain why early runs succeeded (screen awake) but later ones failed only on screenshots while JS execution kept working.

The cascade makes sense too: the previous test aborted before cleaning up outline mode on README, leaving that state active, so when the next test created a note, the marker query hit the wrong editor context. I can't control the user's display, but I could try adding Chromium flags to the wdio config to prevent renderer throttling when windows are backgrounded or occluded—flags like disabling renderer backgrounding and background timer throttling might help keep the screenshot pipeline responsive even if the display locks.

My plan is to restore the branch, check the wdio config, add those flags, and rerun the spec. If screenshots still timeout, I'll accept it as a known artifact like the 5.7 precedent (tests pass fresh, CI is the arbiter) and move forward with the remaining tasks, running the full suite at the end and documenting any screenshot timeouts.

The pre-chevron commit fails identically, confirming the failures are environmental rather than code-related, so I'll update my doc comment to remove the reproduction claim while keeping the preventive hardening. The timeouts are isolated to screenshot capture, not the renderer, pointing to an issue with the capture pipeline itself.

input.old_string:
* The property is written to `view.dom` (the outer `.cm-editor`), NOT
   * `view.contentDOM`, and only when the (rounded) value actually changed —
   * both parts load-bearing, not style. CM6's DOMObserver observes
   * `contentDOM` itself with `attributes: true` (confirmed in
   * @codemirror/view's source), so a style-attribute write there lands
   * inside the observed set; and this measurement is a rect difference on a
   * transformed element, which can jitter subpixel between renders. The
   * combination — a value that keeps changing, written to an observed
   * attribute, from a hook the resulting mutation re-triggers — is the same
   * mutation-observer feedback-loop family as the module doc comment's
   * "never append a child into a plain `.cm-line`" invariant, and it
   * reproduced as a pegged renderer (chromedriver screenshot timeouts)
   * before this was fixed. Custom properties inherit, so `view.dom` serves
   * the descendant chevron rule just as well.
   */

   * both preventive, chosen deliberately rather than in response to an

   * `attributes: true` (confirmed in @codemirror/view's source), so a
   * style-attribute write there lands inside the observed set; and this
   * measurement is a rect difference on a transformed element, which could
   * in principle jitter subpixel between renders. A value that keeps
   * changing, written to an observed attribute, from a hook the resulting
   * mutation might re-trigger, is the same mutation-observer feedback-loop
   * family as the module doc comment's "never append a child into a plain
   * `.cm-line`" invariant — staying outside the observed subtree removes
   * the question entirely, at zero cost (custom properties inherit, so
   * `view.dom` serves the descendant chevron rule just as well).
   */

content.subject: 5.4 Consolidate to one shared parse()/decorate() pass
content.description: Refactor decorations.ts so the three ViewPlugins share one cached parse/decorate/computeLineGuides computation per doc (WeakMap on state.doc). No behavior change; full suite is the safety net.

content.subject: 5.1 Live-measure fold-chevron constants
content.description: Replace 0.425rem/3px hardcoded constants in styles.css chevron translateX with live measurement (glyph, not wrapper), following MarginCompensation's nativeMarginBasePx pattern. Reach stays unconditional on headings.

content.subject: 5.3 Evaluate updateOptions() vs forceRedraw
content.description: Try app.workspace.updateOptions() in setMarkerVisibility; bar = table-only note's marker updates without mode toggle (existing e2e tests cover it). Replace if it passes; else document and keep hack. Table-only regression test stays either way.

content.subject: 5.5 Adopt declarative settings API
content.description: getSettingDefinitions() + getControlValue/setControlValue on TrueOutlinerSettingTab; clears the last lint warning.

content.subject: 5.6 aria-hidden + RTL/IME/theme verification passes
content.description: aria-hidden="true" on marker SVGs; verification (not necessarily fixes) for RTL, IME composition at line start, 2-3 community themes; record findings as tasks/research note, fix only cheap+safe.
content.activeForm: Polishing accessibility and verifying edge contexts

content.subject: 5.2 Guard the two DOM invariants (lint rule)
content.description: Protect (a) widget-subtree injection assumption, (b) never append into plain .cm-line — via a scoped no-restricted-syntax lint rule for decorations.ts with sanctioned call sites explicitly disabled inline.

content.subject: 4.1 + 4.2 Traceability e2e tests
content.description: 4.1: e2e test that opens a table cell for editing and asserts the nested editor carries no decoration state. 4.2: e2e test snapshotting cursor + undo depth across a decoration recompute (mode toggle), asserting no mutation.

content.subject: 5.8 Restate accepted design costs in design.md
content.description: Add accepted costs (gutter shift on toggle, relaxed Experiment-1 invariants) to design.md Risks so they aren't rediscovered as bugs; check off task.

content.subject: Final verification + draft PR
content.description: build:plugin, full desktop e2e, mobile e2e (triage per buckets), screenshots both themes, lint 0 errors, update doc 10 checklist statuses, tasks.md checkoffs, draft PR to main flagging manual real-vault pass.

git add e2e/wdio.conf.mts e2e/wdio.mobile-emulation.conf.mts && git commit --quiet -m "test(e2e): disable Chromium background-window throttling to fix screenshot timeouts

Root cause of the documented screenshot flake (tasks.md 5.7): with the test
window occluded or the display asleep, Chromium throttles frame production,
so saveScreenshot (which needs a fresh frame) times out at chromedriver's
10s renderer-message limit while executeObsidian script round-trips keep
passing. Reproduced deterministically against code that had passed the same
spec minutes earlier; eliminated completely by the three standard
anti-throttling flags in both wdio configs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
git add src/plugin/decorations.ts e2e/specs/52-block-markers-icons.e2e.ts && git commit --quiet -m "refactor(decorations): write chevron measurement props outside CM6's observed subtree

CM6's DOMObserver watches contentDOM itself with attributes: true, so the
per-render style-attribute writes for --to-chevron-dead-right /
--to-marker-icon-size now target view.dom (the outer .cm-editor, outside
the observed set) instead, with a rounded value and a change guard. A
repeatedly-changing value written to an observed attribute from a hook the
resulting mutation might re-trigger is the same mutation-observer
feedback-loop family as the documented .cm-line invariant; writing outside
the subtree removes the question at zero cost (custom properties inherit).
Preventive hardening, not a fix for an observed failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
git add -A && git commit --quiet -m "docs(decorations): evaluate updateOptions() for the settings refresh - rejected with evidence

Hardening 5.3: app.workspace.updateOptions() swapped in for forceRedraw
fails exactly the scenario the hack exists for - a markerVisibility change
on a table-only note (byte-identical decoration output) left the
widget-atom marker stale, because a reconfigure with no decoration diff
never fires MarginCompensation.docViewUpdate. obsidian-lapel's usage works
only because it swaps its extension array entry in place (a real
reconfigure diff); ours reads the setting live. Recorded in forceRedraw's
doc comment so it isn't re-tried; the table-only e2e tests remain the
regression net. tasks.md 5.7 updated with the screenshot-flake root cause.
